content.text: work on master only ok - anyway [Image #1] Why is PX V1 apps showing twice in my sidebar? I just reinstalled the latest version of SuperSet, and now this is happening. Also, I can't seem to snooze certain projects or work trees on the side. Also, for all workspaces, the master should be at worst archived. The master should never be deleted. addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- codex:codex-rescue: Proactively use when Claude Code is stuck, wants a second implementation or diagnosis pass, needs a deeper root-cause investigation, or should hand a substantial coding task to Codex through the shared runtime (Tools: Bash)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- feature-dev:code-architect: Designs feature architectures by analyzing existing codebase patterns and conventions, then providing comprehensive implementation blueprints with specific files to create/modify, component designs, data flows, and build sequences (Tools: Glob, Grep, LS, Read, NotebookRead, WebFetch, TodoWrite, WebSearch, KillShell, BashOutput)

- feature-dev:code-explorer: Deeply analyzes existing codebase features by tracing execution paths, mapping architecture layers, understanding patterns and abstractions, and documenting dependencies to inform new development (Tools: Glob, Grep, LS, Read, NotebookRead, WebFetch, TodoWrite, WebSearch, KillShell, BashOutput)

- feature-dev:code-reviewer: Reviews code for bugs, logic errors, security vulnerabilities, code quality issues, and adherence to project conventions, using confidence-based filtering to report only high-priority issues that truly matter (Tools: Glob, Grep, LS, Read, NotebookRead, WebFetch, TodoWrite, WebSearch, KillShell, BashOutput)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- project-structure-validator: Validates project structure against co-location and architecture patterns defined in AGENTS.md (Tools: All tools)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- understand-anything:architecture-analyzer: Analyzes a codebase's file structure, summaries, and import relationships to identify
logical architectural layers and assign every file to exactly one layer. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:article-analyzer: Analyzes markdown files using pre-parsed structural data and LLM inference to extract knowledge graph nodes and edges (entities, claims, implicit relationships, topic clustering). (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:assemble-reviewer: Reviews the output of merge-batch-graphs.py for semantic issues the script
cannot catch. Recovers dropped nodes/edges and fills cross-batch gaps. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:domain-analyzer: Analyzes codebases to extract business domain knowledge — domains, business flows, and process steps. Produces a domain-graph.json that maps how business logic flows through the code. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:file-analyzer: Analyzes batches of source files to produce knowledge graph nodes and edges.
Extracts file structure, functions, classes, and relationships using a two-phase
approach: structural extraction script followed by LLM semantic analysis. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:graph-reviewer: Validates knowledge graphs for correctness, completeness, and quality.
Runs systematic checks and renders approval or rejection decisions. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:knowledge-graph-guide: Use this agent when users need help understanding, querying, or working
with an Understand-Anything knowledge graph. Guides users through graph
structure, node/edge relationships, layer architecture, tours, and
dashboard usage. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:project-scanner: Scans a codebase directory to produce a structured inventory of all project files,
detected languages, frameworks, import maps, and estimated complexity. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:tour-builder: Designs guided learning tours through codebases, creating 5-15 pedagogical steps
that teach project architecture and key concepts in logical order. (Tools: All tools)

attachment.content:
- access-new-relic-logs: Use when you or the user wants to investigate logs for app.
- access-outlook-business-email: Use when you or the user wants to read, search, triage, or reason about a Microsoft 365 business mailbox, or draft a reply or new email for the user to review and send. This skill never sends — it only creates drafts.
- access-xero-for-px: Full Xero API control for the "Px Internal" Custom Connection (machine-to-machine, single org Pharmacy-Xpress). Use when you or the user wants to read or write Xero data for the PX organisation — invoices, contacts, payments, items, bank transactions, reports, payroll, files, assets. Authenticates via OAuth2 client_credentials and exposes a generic HTTP passthrough to any Xero API endpoint.
- anvil-yaml: Update Anvil app manifest files in `anvil.yaml`, including services, app metadata, and `db_schema`, using the Anvil YAML type reference available to this agent and `anvil --json validate`.
- brand-guidelines: Applies Anthropic's official brand colors and typography to any sort of artifact that may benefit from having Anthropic's look-and-feel. Use it when brand colors or style guidelines, visual formatting, or company design standards apply.
- call-another-llm: Call Codex CLI, Claude Code CLI, or Gemini CLI with a prompt and save the response to tmp/llm-responses/. Use when you need a second opinion, want to offload a subtask, or compare answers across LLMs.
- canvas-design: Create beautiful visual art in .png and .pdf documents using design philosophy. You should use this skill when the user asks to create a poster, piece of art, design, or other static piece. Create original visual designs, never copying existing artists' work to avoid copyright violations.
- chatgpt-browser-image-generation: Generate images through the user's already signed-in ChatGPT browser session, with optional brand/reference image upload. Use whenever the user asks an agent to "make an image with ChatGPT", "use ChatGPT to generate <N> images", produce branded artwork, mock-ups, icons, hero illustrations, or social assets through their ChatGPT subscription instead of an image API. Open one tab per distinct request and run them in parallel; if requests are variations of the same content, keep them in a single chat. Downloaded images land in the user's Downloads folder and the script moves them into the working directory.
- chatgpt-pro-browser-query: Query ChatGPT through the user's already signed-in browser session while proving ChatGPT Pro and Extended Pro are active before sending. Use this whenever the user asks an agent to ask ChatGPT, use ChatGPT Pro, compare with ChatGPT, query the ChatGPT web app, or get a second opinion specifically from their ChatGPT subscription instead of the API.
- chrome-control: Shared Chrome DevTools control bridge that owns the single CDP connection so multiple Chrome-using skills run from one Chrome "Allow control" approval. Use as STEP 1 of any skill that drives the user's Chrome (ChatGPT browser query, ChatGPT image generation, future browser-driving skills).
- chrome-devtools-cli: Use this skill to write shell scripts or run shell commands to automate tasks in the browser or otherwise use Chrome DevTools via CLI.
- claude-epic-pr-review: Token-intensive, maximal review of a large pull request or branch. Runs three parallel review streams — a 5-agent Codex xhigh swarm with overlapping coverage, an adversarial multi-agent epic-review workflow, and a fork that validates every existing PR comment after checks pass — then consolidates all findings, auto-fixes valid code bugs in parallel while asking the user about UX / DB-schema / new-file decisions, runs /code-review max + /simplify max, and replaces the PR. Use for large, high-risk, or cross-cutting changes when an exhaustive, deliberately expensive review is wanted.
- claude-team-review-plan-implementation: Launch a large team of agents (Codex, Gemini, Opus teammates) to line-by-line compare a reference plan against its implementation across multiple repos, extract deviations, validate, fix, verify, triage design decisions with the user, simplify, and create PRs. Accepts a reference file name as its argument.
- client-modules: Edit Anvil client code Python modules under `client_code` that are not form definitions, without touching read-only dependencies or form templates.
- doc-coauthoring: Guide users through a structured workflow for co-authoring documentation. Use when user wants to write documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision docs, or similar structured content. This workflow helps users efficiently transfer context, refine content through iteration, and verify the doc works for readers. Trigger when user mentions writing docs, creating proposals, drafting specs, or similar documentation tasks.
- docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- form-code: Edit Anvil form Python under `client_code`, including handlers, lifecycle, and the required form class that inherits from its designer template.
- form-html-template: Create, inspect, and edit Anvil forms saved as HTML templates, including package `form_template.html` files and module-form `.html` files beside `.py` files.
- form-styling: Style Anvil form UI using component properties, roles, CSS classes, theme CSS, and M3 guidance.
- form-yaml-template: Convert legacy Anvil form template YAML to HTML with `anvil convert-template` only for template/layout work after confirming no sibling HTML exists. The command removes the source YAML after successful conversion by default. Hand-edit YAML only when the user explicitly asks to stay on YAML.
- frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- heavy-swarm-plan: Build a read-only implementation plan for complex, multi-area work and pressure-test that plan with Codex CLI, Claude Code CLI, and Gemini CLI before implementation starts. Use when the task is large, ambiguous, cross-cutting, or risky enough that a normal quick plan is not sufficient.
- implement-good-test-patterns: Use datasette-enrichments as a testing reference and imitate its pytest patterns when adding or restructuring tests. Use when writing tests, improving test structure, or aligning a Python project with good integration-style pytest patterns.
- mailshot-carehomes: Use when you or the user wants to send a single bulk announcement (mailshot) to every care-home portal contact - e.g. a new branding link, a portal change, a service notice. Gathers all care-home recipient emails from the Anvil carehome_portal_users table and the Neon "PX" script-copy table, then drafts ONE email (FROM team@, CC stacey@, BCC every care-home contact + zoe@/paresh@/mandeep@) via the access-outlook-business-email skill. Never sends - it only creates a draft for the user to review and send.
- manage-m365-tenant: Use when you or the user wants to administer the Microsoft 365 tenancy — users, groups, licenses, roles, domains, devices, policies, security alerts, audit logs, SharePoint sites, Teams, and usage reports. Full read/write control via Microsoft Graph.
- mk-feature-plan-mode: Plan-only feature design mode. The user states a feature objective; read-only forks map the existing codebase (data model, screens, modules) while the main agent grills the user via AskUser to lock down every UX edge case and scenario. The locked plan is then reviewed by ~2 parallel Codex agents plus an architecture-review sub-agent; any new gaps loop back to the user. Once locked, it writes a feature_plan_<feature>.html design doc to the cwd (UI screens, schema changes, file-tree of new/changed files, out-of-scope) and opens it in the user's default browser, plus a migration.py (test branch by default, --live for prod) only when the schema changes are not purely additive. Use when the user says "feature plan mode", "plan this feature", "design doc for ...", or wants a reviewable UX/architecture plan before any code is written.
- pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- performance-metrics-from-new-relic: Use when you or the user wants frontend performance metrics from New Relic, including Largest Contentful Paint, Interaction to Next Paint, Cumulative Layout Shift, poor distributions, and worst URLs or sessions.
- pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- pr-swarm-review: Fetch comments from an open GitHub pull request, then use a single sub-agent to extract every reviewer's substantive concerns and validate each one against the codebase in one pass, and consolidate the verdicts back in the main agent for triage and fixing. Use when the user wants existing PR comments triaged before deciding what to fix.
- quick-smoke-tests: Generate an ordered, dependency-aware list of UI smoke tests for the work just completed in this session. Walk the user through every test using the AskUser tool — one question per test with all details embedded in the question — then validate each reported result against the database. Use when the user says "smoke test what we just did", "let's quickly verify the changes in the UI", or similar after implementation work.
- review-with-swarm-agents: Run code review with Codex CLI, Claude Code CLI, and Gemini CLI using read-only prompts over repo diffs. Use when the user asks for a review or before committing substantial changes. If this skill is invoked then do not commit before running this.
- server-code: Edit Anvil server code Python modules under `server_code`, including server-callable functions, background logic, and server packages, while keeping changes isolated to the app workspace.
- skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- sync-global-user-instructions-md: Sync one canonical global instruction file into user-global Codex, Claude, and Gemini instruction files. Use when you need to update the canonical user-global instructions, diff installed global files against canon, reconcile drift, or replace user-global AGENTS/CLAUDE/GEMINI files from the canonical source.
- sync-mcps: Sync canonical MCP server manifests into Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and Cursor config files. Use when you need to add, remove, or update an MCP across all agents from one source of truth, or to diff installed MCP configs against canon.
- theme-factory: Toolkit for styling artifacts with a theme. These artifacts can be slides, docs, reportings, HTML landing pages, etc. There are 10 pre-set themes with colors/fonts that you can apply to any artifact that has been creating, or can generate a new theme on-the-fly.
- webapp-testing: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.
- windows-computer-use: Drive the Windows desktop UI the way a user can — list monitors/windows, read UIA element trees, click/right-click/double-click, type text, send key chords, scroll, drag, and capture screenshots across multiple monitors and any app type. Use when a task needs native Windows app or OS control beyond the browser (File Explorer, Settings, installers, system dialogs, Office desktop, WinUI/UWP/Win32 apps). For controlling a web browser, prefer chrome-control / the browser skills instead.
- xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- feature-dev:feature-dev: Guided feature development with codebase understanding and architecture focus
- astral:ruff: Guide for using ruff, the extremely fast Python linter and formatter. Use this when linting, formatting, or fixing Python code.
- astral:ty: Guide for using ty, the extremely fast Python type checker and language server. Use this when type checking Python code or setting up type checking in Python projects.
- astral:uv: Guide for using uv, the Python package and project manager. Use this when working with Python projects, scripts, packages, or tools.
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Guidance for distinctive, intentional visual design when building new UI or reshaping an existing one. Helps with aesthetic direction, typography, and making choices that don't read as templated defaults.
- understand-anything:understand: Analyze a codebase to produce an interactive knowledge graph for understanding architecture, components, and relationships
- understand-anything:understand-chat: Use when you need to ask questions about a codebase or understand code using a knowledge graph
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard: Launch the interactive web dashboard to visualize a codebase's knowledge graph
- understand-anything:understand-diff: Use when you need to analyze git diffs or pull requests to understand what changed, affected components, and risks
- understand-anything:understand-domain: Extract business domain knowledge from a codebase and generate an interactive domain flow graph. Works standalone (lightweight scan) or derives from an existing /understand knowledge graph.
- understand-anything:understand-explain: Use when you need a deep-dive explanation of a specific file, function, or module in the codebase
- understand-anything:understand-knowledge: Analyze a Karpathy-pattern LLM wiki knowledge base and generate an interactive knowledge graph with entity extraction, implicit relationships, and topic clustering.
- understand-anything:understand-onboard: Use when you need to generate an onboarding guide for new team members joining a project
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

export const meta = {
  name: 'sidebar-triage',
  description: 'Investigate duplicate project rows, snooze gating, main-workspace delete paths, and live local data',
  phases: [
    { title: 'Investigate', detail: '4 parallel readers: project creation, snooze gating, delete paths, live data' },
  ],
}

phase('Investigate')

const REPORT = {
  type: 'object',
  properties: {
    findings: { type: 'string', description: 'Detailed findings with file:line references' },
    keyFacts: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Short bullet facts, each with file:line where applicable' },
  },
  required: ['findings', 'keyFacts'],
}

const cwd = 'C:/Users/khair/.superset/worktrees/d7ac4c2f-2b74-4e23-a35c-f6c443cf7cf5/muddled-cake'

const results = await parallel([
  () => agent(`You are investigating a Superset desktop (Electron) fork at ${cwd}. READ-ONLY — do not edit anything.

QUESTION: A user's sidebar shows the SAME repo project twice: "PX V1 apps" (has 1 branch workspace) and "PX v1 apps" (note lowercase v, count 1, no visible children). This appeared after reinstalling the latest build (upstream desktop-v1.14.2 merged). Find the mechanism that can create two project rows for one local repo.

Investigate:
1. The renderer sidebar reads collections.v2SidebarProjects innerJoin collections.v2Projects (see apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/DashboardSidebar/hooks/useDashboardSidebarData/useDashboardSidebarData.ts). Find where these collections are DEFINED (CollectionsProvider) — which are cloud-synced (Electric) vs device-local persisted, and where local ones persist on disk.
2. Find where a v2Projects row and a v2SidebarProjects row get CREATED for a local repo/folder: the "add workspace"/project-registration flow (renderer + host-service). What is the match/dedupe key when deciding whether a project already exists for a repo — name? slug? path? case-sensitive? Look for code that, on opening/importing a repo, either reuses an existing project or creates a new one.
3. How could a reinstall produce a duplicate: e.g. sidebar-local rows wiped but cloud project rows kept, then re-import created a NEW project row instead of matching the old one (case-sensitive name/slug compare? path stored where?). Check whether project name is derived from folder basename and whether the folder name case matters.
4. git log/recent upstream changes (v1.14.x merge e376fbf79) touching project creation/matching/slug — anything that changed the dedupe key.
5. Also note: is there any code path that HIDES a project row with zero visible children, or is every v2SidebarProjects row always rendered?

Return findings with exact file:line references and the precise dedupe key used at each creation site.`, { label: 'dup-project-mechanism', phase: 'Investigate', schema: REPORT, effort: 'xhigh' }),

  () => agent(`You are investigating a Superset desktop (Electron) fork at ${cwd}. READ-ONLY — do not edit anything.

QUESTION: User reports "I can't snooze certain projects or worktrees in the sidebar". Build the exact availability matrix for Snooze / Archive / Delete actions across every sidebar row type.

Investigate these files (and whatever they lead to):
- apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/DashboardSidebar/components/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem/components/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceContextMenu/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceContextMenu.tsx
- apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/DashboardSidebar/components/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem/hooks/useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions/useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions.ts
- apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/DashboardSidebar/components/DashboardSidebarProjectSection/components/DashboardSidebarProjectContextMenu/DashboardSidebarProjectContextMenu.tsx
- apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/providers/CollectionsProvider/dashboardSidebarLocal/* (snooze/archive write paths, ensureSidebarWorkspaceRecord, sidebarVisibility)

Answer precisely:
1. For a WORKSPACE row: under which conditions (type === 'main'? hostType remote? no local-state row? pending transaction? host offline?) are Snooze/Archive/Delete hidden, disabled, or no-ops? Cite file:line for each condition.
2. For a PROJECT row (the repo header): is there ANY snooze/archive at project level, or only per-workspace? What does the project context menu offer?
3. Are there row states where the snooze WRITE silently fails — e.g. auto-included local main workspaces that have no v2WorkspaceLocalState row (see isAutoIncludedLocalMainWorkspace) — does ensureSidebarWorkspaceRecord create the row first? Any known gap where clicking Snooze does nothing?
4. The fork memory says a TS2339 'snooze' type error exists somewhere — check if any snooze code path is actually broken at runtime vs just typing.

Return a matrix: rowType/state -> Snooze? Archive? Delete? with file:line evidence.`, { label: 'snooze-gating', phase: 'Investigate', schema: REPORT, effort: 'xhigh' }),

  () => agent(`You are investigating a Superset desktop (Electron) fork at ${cwd}. READ-ONLY — do not edit anything.

QUESTION: User wants: "for all repos, the MAIN/master workspace must never be deletable — at worst archivable". Map the CURRENT delete + archive behavior for main-type workspaces.

Context: the fork has a Recycle Bin (soft-delete) feature — every delete entry point routes to soft-delete (deletedAt), permanent delete only inside the bin. Fork marker (RECYCLE-BIN).

Investigate:
1. Enumerate ALL delete entry points for a workspace (sidebar context menu, project context menu 'remove project'?, kanban card delete, workspace page, command palette, keyboard shortcut, recycle-bin permanent delete). For each: can it target a type === 'main' workspace today? Cite file:line.
2. What does deleting a MAIN workspace actually do (destroy worktree? just hide row? remove project?)? Distinguish 'remove from sidebar' vs 'delete workspace' vs 'delete project'.
3. Current archive behavior for main workspaces: the bucket classifier comment says 'a master card removed now archives too (archivedAt)'. Find getWorkspaceSidebarBucket + ensureSidebarWorkspaceRecord in apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/providers/CollectionsProvider/dashboardSidebarLocal/ and explain exactly how a main workspace ends up archived vs hidden vs deleted today.
4. Where would a 'main workspaces: no delete, archive allowed' guard need to live so ALL entry points are covered (UI hide + a hard guard at the mutation layer)? List the exact functions.
5. Also check the kanban board rules: memory says 'main workspaces can't be snoozed/archived/deleted' there — confirm and cite.

Return the entry-point list with current main-workspace behavior at each, and the minimal set of guard locations.`, { label: 'main-delete-paths', phase: 'Investigate', schema: REPORT, effort: 'xhigh' }),

  () => agent(`You are on the user's Windows machine investigating LIVE Superset desktop app data. READ-ONLY: never modify, delete, or write any app data file. The app is RUNNING right now — open files read-only/immutable only. Repo (for code reference) at ${cwd}.

GOAL: find the concrete data behind a sidebar bug: project "PX V1 apps" appears TWICE (second instance named "PX v1 apps" with lowercase v). Locate the actual stored rows.

Steps:
1. Find where the desktop app persists renderer collection data: check code in apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/providers/CollectionsProvider (esp. dashboardSidebarLocal) and packages/local-db for the on-disk location (likely under C:/Users/khair/.superset or %APPDATA%/superset or %APPDATA%/Superset). List candidate dirs/files (dir listings are fine).
2. Find the sqlite/libsql/json files holding: v2Projects (may be cloud-sync cache), v2SidebarProjects, v2WorkspaceLocalState, and host-service workspace/project registry (~/.superset/*.json? sqlite?). Also check ~/.superset for JSON registries like superset.json, workspaces.json, projects.json, multi-repo/.
3. Extract every row whose name matches PX v1/V1 (case-insensitive 'px v1' / 'px-v1' / similar): project id, name, slug, createdAt, any path field; and the workspaces attached to each (id, name, branch, type, path). Use 'uv run python' with sqlite3 stdlib (open with file: URI ?mode=ro&immutable=1) for sqlite files; plain reads for JSON. If a file is a non-sqlite format (e.g. IndexedDB/LevelDB under %APPDATA%), say so and extract what's greppable (careful: binary — use python, not shell cat).
4. Also extract rows for ALL projects (id+name+slug only) so we can see the full sidebar list, and any snooze/archive/deleted state rows (v2WorkspaceLocalState sidebarState) for the PX workspaces.
5. Note file paths of everything you found.

Return: the two PX project rows (full fields), their attached workspaces, where each row lives on disk, and the full project id+name list.`, { label: 'live-data', phase: 'Investigate', schema: REPORT, effort: 'xhigh' }),
])

const [dup, snooze, del, live] = results
return {
  duplicateMechanism: dup,
  snoozeGating: snooze,
  mainDeletePaths: del,
  liveData: live,
}

<summary>Dynamic workflow "Investigate duplicate project rows, snooze gating, main-workspace delete paths, and live local data" completed</summary>
<result>{"duplicateMechanism":{"findings":"## 1. Collection definitions (CollectionsProvider)

File: `C:\Users\khair\.superset\worktrees\d7ac4c2f-2b74-4e23-a35c-f6c443cf7cf5\muddled-cake\apps\desktop\src\renderer\routes\_authenticated\providers\CollectionsProvider\collections.ts`

- `v2Projects` — CLOUD-synced Electric shape on Postgres table `v2_projects` (lines 349-380), wrapped in `createPersistedElectricCollection` → persisted to a renderer-side SQLite cache via `createElectronSQLitePersistence` (lines 100-102, 116-128). Cloud DB is source of truth; rows survive any reinstall.
- `v2SidebarProjects` — DEVICE-LOCAL `localStorageCollectionOptions`, storageKey `v2-sidebar-projects-${organizationId}`, key = `item.projectId` (lines 784-791). Lives in the renderer's `window.localStorage` → Electron userData dir (`%APPDATA%\Superset\...\Local Storage`). NOT cloud-synced.
- `v2WorkspaceLocalState` (per-workspace sidebar placement) — also localStorage, storageKey `v2-workspace-local-state-${organizationId}` (lines 797-811).
- Third store: host-service local SQLite `host.db` at `~/.superset/host/<organizationId>/host.db` (`apps/desktop/src/main/lib/host-service-coordinator.ts:482`, `host-service-manifest.ts:19-21`, `app-environment.ts:8-9`). Its `projects` table (`packages/host-service/src/db/schema.ts:57`) is the ONLY place `repoPath` is stored — the cloud `v2_projects` table has NO path column (`packages/db/src/schema/schema.ts:402-429`).
- NSIS installer (`apps/desktop/electron-builder.ts:185-193`) does NOT set `deleteAppDataOnUninstall`; both `%APPDATA%` and `~/.superset` normally survive reinstall.

Sidebar render = `v2SidebarProjects` innerJoin `v2Projects` (`useDashboardSidebarData.ts:157-192`). A project row renders iff a localStorage membership row exists AND the cloud project row exists.

## 2. Creation flow + the dedupe key at each site

Renderer folder import: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/AddRepositoryModals/hooks/useFolderFirstImport/useFolderFirstImport.ts`
- line 58: `client.project.findByPath.query({ repoPath })` — the ONLY dedupe probe.
- lines 98-107: if exactly one candidate → `project.setup` (reuse).
- lines 115-120: if ZERO candidates → `project.create` with `name: getBaseName(repoPath)` (line 66/116) — **project name = folder basename, case-preserved from the OS picker path** (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/lib/pathBasename/pathBasename.ts:1-3`).
- After create/setup: `useFinalizeProjectSetup.ts:22-32` → `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar`/`ensureProjectInSidebar` → `ensureSidebarProjectRecord` inserts the `v2SidebarProjects` row (`useDashboardSidebarState.ts:125-141, 219-232`).

Host-service dedupe probe `project.findByPath` (`packages/host-service/src/trpc/router/project/project.ts:154-412`):
- **Key 1 (local, folder-first default)**: exact string equality on the host's local sqlite — `ctx.db.query.projects.findFirst({ where: eq(projects.repoPath, gitRoot) })` (lines 231-233; non-git variant line 183-185). SQLite TEXT with default BINARY collation → **CASE-SENSITIVE full-path compare**. `gitRoot` comes from `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` (`utils/resolve-repo.ts:143-151, 169-183`), i.e. current on-disk casing. A case-only folder rename (PX V1 apps → PX v1 apps) makes the stored row unfindable.
- **Key 2 (cloud fallback)**: only if the repo has a **parseable GitHub remote** — `v2Project.findByGitHubRemote` compares `lower(repoCloneUrl)` case-insensitively (`packages/trpc/src/router/v2-project/v2-project.ts:126-167`, esp. 140-144, 161). Repo with no remote, or non-GitHub remote (e.g. an Anvil remote) → `if (!parsed) return { candidates: [] }` (project.ts:254-255) → **no cloud dedupe at all**.
- **Name and slug are NEVER used as a dedupe key.**

Host-service create (`packages/host-service/src/trpc/router/project/handlers.ts`):
- `createFromImportLocal` (239-254) / `createFromNonGitFolder` (263-275) → `persistFromResolved` (120-203): inserts local `projects` row keyed by fresh `randomUUID()` (line 138) with `repoPath` (`utils/persist-project.ts:5-23`), then cloud `v2Project.create`.
- `createCloudProjectWithSlugRetry` (74-107): slug = `slugifyProjectName(name)` — **lowercased** basename (lines 26-39, so "PX V1 apps" and "PX v1 apps" both → `px-v1-apps`). On the `v2_projects_org_slug_unique` CONFLICT ("Project slug already exists", v2-project.ts:261-267) it does **NOT reuse the existing project — it retries with `px-v1-apps-2`, `-3` … up to 100** (lines 81-101) and silently mints a SECOND cloud project row. This is THE duplicate-manufacturing site.
- Cloud `v2Project.create` itself (`v2-project.ts:169-317`) is a dumb insert; DB uniqueness is only `unique("v2_projects_org_slug_unique").on(organizationId, slug)` (`packages/db/src/schema/schema.ts:427`) — no uniqueness on name or repoCloneUrl ("Multiple v2 projects may point at the same GitHub URL" is explicitly allowed, project.ts:148-151).
- `createFromMultiRepo` (288-366): **zero dedupe of any kind** — always `randomUUID()` + fresh anchor dir `~/.superset/multi-repo/<projectId>`; its anchor `repoPath` never matches any member repo path, so `findByPath` on a member can never find it. Re-creating the same multi-folder group = guaranteed second project. (No main workspace → sidebar row via `ensureProjectInSidebar` only, project.ts / handlers.ts:153-160.)
- Second renderer path `useEnsureV2Project.ts:31-62` has the same shape: `findByPath` → fall through to `create` on zero candidates.

## 3. How reinstall surfaces/creates the duplicate

Mechanism A — duplicate row minted at re-import: if the host `projects.repoPath` row is missing or mismatching (host.db wiped/moved, org id changed — the DB is per-org `~/.superset/host/<orgId>/host.db`; OR case-only folder rename breaking the BINARY compare) AND the repo has no parseable GitHub remote, `findByPath` returns 0 candidates → renderer creates a brand-new project named after the folder basename's CURRENT casing. Old cloud row "PX V1 apps" (slug `px-v1-apps`) persists; new one becomes "PX v1 apps" (slug `px-v1-apps-2` via the retry loop). Both innerJoin-render once each has a `v2SidebarProjects` row.

Mechanism B — pre-existing duplicate resurrected by the v1.14 auto-placement change: `usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar` (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/components/AgentHooks/hooks/usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar/usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar.ts:51-63` + `selectWorktreesToPlace.ts:18-28`) runs on startup and, for every LOCAL `worktree`-type workspace with no `v2WorkspaceLocalState` row, calls `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar(worktree.id, worktree.projectId)` — which INSERTS a `v2SidebarProjects` row for that projectId (`useDashboardSidebarState.ts:226-232`). In the v1.14.0 merge (commit `1e89c577b` "offline-first host-owned workspace table", contained in `2b0a3ef44`/`e376fbf79`), this hook's source switched from the cloud `v2Workspaces` Electric collection to `useHostWorkspaces()` (host.db, host-owned). So after installing the v1.14.2 build, any worktree rows sitting in `~/.superset/host/<org>/host.db` under an old duplicate projectId — invisible before — get auto-placed and the duplicate project row appears in the sidebar without any user action. If the renderer localStorage was reset (fresh `%APPDATA%` profile / uninstall with data removal), ALL placement tombstones are gone and every host worktree re-places, resurrecting every project that has a worktree, including stale duplicates.

Name-from-basename confirmation: yes — `useFolderFirstImport.ts:66,116` uses `getBaseName(repoPath)`; folder-name case directly becomes the project name; slug lowercases it so case-variants collide on slug and trigger the suffix-retry duplicate instead of a reuse or a loud error.

## 4. Upstream v1.14.x changes

- `git diff da244dbca(v1.13) e376fbf79(v1.14.2)`: NO change to the dedupe keys — `findByPath`, `createCloudProjectWithSlugRetry

Its `projects` table (`packages/host-service/src/db/schema.ts:57`) is the ONLY place `repoPath` is stored — the cloud `v2_projects` table has NO path column (`packages/db/src/schema/schema.ts:402-429`).

## 2. Creation flow + the dedupe key at each site

Renderer folder import: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/AddRepositoryModals/hooks/useFolderFirstImport/useFolderFirstImport.ts`
- line 58: `client.project.findByPath.query({ repoPath })` — the ONLY dedupe probe.
- lines 98-107: if exactly one candidate → `project.setup` (reuse).
- lines 115-120: if ZERO candidates → `project.create` with `name: getBaseName(repoPath)` (line 66/116) — **project name = folder basename, case-preserved from the OS picker path** (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/lib/pathBasename/pathBasename.ts:1-3`).
- After create/setup: `useFinalizeProjectSetup.ts:22-32` → `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar`/`ensureProjectInSidebar` → `ensureSidebarProjectRecord` inserts the `v2SidebarProjects` row (`useDashboardSidebarState.ts:125-141, 219-232`).

Host-service dedupe probe `project.findByPath` (`packages/host-service/src/trpc/router/project/project.ts:154-412`):
- **Key 1 (local, folder-first default)**: exact string equality on the host's local sqlite — `ctx.db.query.projects.findFirst({ where: eq(projects.repoPath, gitRoot) })` (lines 231-233; non-git variant line 183-185). SQLite TEXT with default BINARY collation → **CASE-SENSITIVE full-path compare**. `gitRoot` comes from `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` (`utils/resolve-repo.ts:143-151, 169-183`), i.e. current on-disk casing. A case-only folder rename (PX V1 apps → PX v1 apps) makes the stored row unfindable.
- **Key 2 (cloud fallback)**: only if the repo has a **parseable GitHub remote** — `v2Project.findByGitHubRemote` compares `lower(repoCloneUrl)` case-insensitively (`packages/trpc/src/router/v2-project/v2-project.ts:126-167`, esp. 140-144, 161). Repo with no remote, or non-GitHub remote (e.g. an Anvil remote) → `if (!parsed) return { candidates: [] }` (project.ts:254-255) → **no cloud dedupe at all**.
- **Name and slug are NEVER used as a dedupe key.**

Host-service create (`packages/host-service/src/trpc/router/project/handlers.ts`):
- `createFromImportLocal` (239-254) / `createFromNonGitFolder` (263-275) → `persistFromResolved` (120-203): inserts local `projects` row keyed by fresh `randomUUID()` (line 138) with `repoPath` (`utils/persist-project.ts:5-23`), then cloud `v2Project.create`.
- `createCloudProjectWithSlugRetry` (74-107): slug = `slugifyProjectName(name)` — **lowercased** basename (lines 26-39, so "PX V1 apps" and "PX v1 apps" both → `px-v1-apps`). On the `v2_projects_org_slug_unique` CONFLICT ("Project slug already exists", v2-project.ts:261-267) it does **NOT reuse the existing project — it retries with `px-v1-apps-2`, `-3` … up to 100** (lines 81-101) and silently mints a SECOND cloud project row. This is THE duplicate-manufacturing site.
- Cloud `v2Project.create` itself (`v2-project.ts:169-317`) is a dumb insert; DB uniqueness is only `unique("v2_projects_org_slug_unique").on(organizationId, slug)` (`packages/db/src/schema/schema.ts:427`) — no uniqueness on name or repoCloneUrl ("Multiple v2 projects may point at the same GitHub URL" is explicitly allowed, project.ts:148-151).
- `createFromMultiRepo` (288-366): **zero dedupe of any kind** — always `randomUUID()` + fresh anchor dir `~/.superset/multi-repo/<projectId>`; its anchor `repoPath` never matches any member repo path, so `findByPath` on a member can never find it. Re-creating the same multi-folder group = guaranteed second project. (No main workspace → sidebar row via `ensureProjectInSidebar` only, project.ts / handlers.ts:153-160.)
- Second renderer path `useEnsureV2Project.ts:31-62` has the same shape: `findByPath` → fall through to `create` on zero candidates.

## 3. How reinstall surfaces/creates the duplicate

Mechanism A — duplicate row minted at re-import: if the host `projects.repoPath` row is missing or mismatching (host.db wiped/moved, org id changed — the DB is per-org `~/.superset/host/<orgId>/host.db`; OR case-only folder rename breaking the BINARY compare) AND the repo has no parseable GitHub remote, `findByPath` returns 0 candidates → renderer creates a brand-new project named after the folder basename's CURRENT casing. Old cloud row "PX V1 apps" (slug `px-v1-apps`) persists; new one becomes "PX v1 apps" (slug `px-v1-apps-2` via the retry loop). Both innerJoin-render once each has a `v2SidebarProjects` row.

Mechanism B — pre-existing duplicate resurrected by the v1.14 auto-placement change: `usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar` (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/components/AgentHooks/hooks/usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar/usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar.ts:51-63` + `selectWorktreesToPlace.ts:18-28`) runs on startup and, for every LOCAL `worktree`-type workspace with no `v2WorkspaceLocalState` row, calls `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar(worktree.id, worktree.projectId)` — which INSERTS a `v2SidebarProjects` row for that projectId (`useDashboardSidebarState.ts:226-232`). In the v1.14.0 merge (commit `1e89c577b` "offline-first host-owned workspace table", contained in `2b0a3ef44`/`e376fbf79`), this hook's source switched from the cloud `v2Workspaces` Electric collection to `useHostWorkspaces()` (host.db, host-owned). So after installing the v1.14.2 build, any worktree rows sitting in `~/.superset/host/<org>/host.db` under an old duplicate projectId — invisible before — get auto-placed and the duplicate project row appears in the sidebar without any user action. If the renderer localStorage was reset (fresh `%APPDATA%` profile / uninstall with data removal), ALL placement tombstones are gone and every host worktree re-places, resurrecting every project that has a worktree, including stale duplicates.

Name-from-basename confirmation: yes — `useFolderFirstImport.ts:66,116` uses `getBaseName(repoPath)`; folder-name case directly becomes the project name; slug lowercases it so case-variants collide on slug and trigger the suffix-retry duplicate instead of a reuse or a loud error.

task.description: Investigate duplicate project rows, snooze gating, main-workspace delete paths, and live local data
task.output:
{
  "summary": "Investigate duplicate project rows, snooze gating, main-workspace delete paths, and live local data",
  "agentCount": 4,
  "logs": [],
  "result": {
    "duplicateMechanism": {
      "findings": "## 1. Collection definitions (CollectionsProvider)

File: `C:\Users\khair\.superset\worktrees\d7ac4c2f-2b74-4e23-a35c-f6c443cf7cf5\muddled-cake\apps\desktop\src\renderer\routes\_authenticated\providers\CollectionsProvider\collections.ts`

- `v2Projects` — CLOUD-synced Electric shape on Postgres table `v2_projects` (lines 349-380), wrapped in `createPersistedElectricCollection` → persisted to a renderer-side SQLite cache via `createElectronSQLitePersistence` (lines 100-102, 116-128). Cloud DB is source of truth; rows survive any reinstall.
- `v2SidebarProjects` — DEVICE-LOCAL `localStorageCollectionOptions`, storageKey `v2-sidebar-projects-${organizationId}`, key = `item.projectId` (lines 784-791). Lives in the renderer's `window.localStorage` → Electron userData dir (`%APPDATA%\Superset\...\Local Storage`). NOT cloud-synced.
- `v2WorkspaceLocalState` (per-workspace sidebar placement) — also localStorage, storageKey `v2-workspace-local-state-${organizationId}` (lines 797-811).
- Third store: host-service local SQLite `host.db` at `~/.superset/host/<organizationId>/host.db` (`apps/desktop/src/main/lib/host-service-coordinator.ts:482`, `host-service-manifest.ts:19-21`, `app-environment.ts:8-9`). Its `projects` table (`packages/host-service/src/db/schema.ts:57`) is the ONLY place `repoPath` is stored — the cloud `v2_projects` table has NO path column (`packages/db/src/schema/schema.ts:402-429`).
- NSIS installer (`apps/desktop/electron-builder.ts:185-193`) does NOT set `deleteAppDataOnUninstall`; both `%APPDATA%` and `~/.superset` normally survive reinstall.

Sidebar render = `v2SidebarProjects` innerJoin `v2Projects` (`useDashboardSidebarData.ts:157-192`). A project row renders iff a localStorage membership row exists AND the cloud project row exists.

## 2. Creation flow + the dedupe key at each site

Renderer folder import: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/AddRepositoryModals/hooks/useFolderFirstImport/useFolderFirstImport.ts`
- line 58: `client.project.findByPath.query({ repoPath })` — the ONLY dedupe probe.
- lines 98-107: if exactly one candidate → `project.setup` (reuse).
- lines 115-120: if ZERO candidates → `project.create` with `name: getBaseName(repoPath)` (line 66/116) — **project name = folder basename, case-preserved from the OS picker path** (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/lib/pathBasename/pathBasename.ts:1-3`).
- After create/setup: `useFinalizeProjectSetup.ts:22-32` → `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar`/`ensureProjectInSidebar` → `ensureSidebarProjectRecord` inserts the `v2SidebarProjects` row (`useDashboardSidebarState.ts:125-141, 219-232`).

Host-service dedupe probe `project.findByPath` (`packages/host-service/src/trpc/router/project/project.ts:154-412`):
- **Key 1 (local, folder-first default)**: exact string equality on the host's local sqlite — `ctx.db.query.projects.findFirst({ where: eq(projects.repoPath, gitRoot) })` (lines 231-233; non-git variant line 183-185). SQLite TEXT with default BINARY collation → **CASE-SENSITIVE full-path compare**. `gitRoot` comes from `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` (`utils/resolve-repo.ts:143-151, 169-183`), i.e. current on-disk casing. A case-only folder rename (PX V1 apps → PX v1 apps) makes the stored row unfindable.
- **Key 2 (cloud fallback)**: only if the repo has a **parseable GitHub remote** — `v2Project.findByGitHubRemote` compares `lower(repoCloneUrl)` case-insensitively (`packages/trpc/src/router/v2-project/v2-project.ts:126-167`, esp. 140-144, 161). Repo with no remote, or non-GitHub remote (e.g. an Anvil remote) → `if (!parsed) return { candidates: [] }` (project.ts:254-255) → **no cloud dedupe at all**.
- **Name and slug are NEVER used as a dedupe key.**

Host-service create (`packages/host-service/src/trpc/router/project/handlers.ts`):
- `createFromImportLocal` (239-254) / `createFromNonGitFolder` (263-275) → `persistFromResolved` (120-203): inserts local `projects` row keyed by fresh `randomUUID()` (line 138) with `repoPath` (`utils/persist-project.ts:5-23`), then cloud `v2Project.create`.
- `createCloudProjectWithSlugRetry` (74-107): slug = `slugifyProjectName(name)` — **lowercased** basename (lines 26-39, so "PX V1 apps" and "PX v1 apps" both → `px-v1-apps`). On the `v2_projects_org_slug_unique` CONFLICT ("Project slug already exists", v2-project.ts:261-267) it does **NOT reuse the existing project — it retries with `px-v1-apps-2`, `-3` … up to 100** (lines 81-101) and silently mints a SECOND cloud project row. This is THE duplicate-manufacturing site.
- Cloud `v2Project.create` itself (`v2-project.ts:169-317`) is a dumb insert; DB uniqueness is only `unique("v2_projects_org_slug_unique").on(organizationId, slug)` (`packages/db/src/schema/schema.ts:427`) — no uniqueness on name or repoCloneUrl ("Multiple v2 projects may point at the same GitHub URL" is explicitly allowed, project.ts:148-151).
- `createFromMultiRepo` (288-366): **zero dedupe of any kind** — always `randomUUID()` + fresh anchor dir `~/.superset/multi-repo/<projectId>`; its anchor `repoPath` never matches any member repo path, so `findByPath` on a member can never find it. Re-creating the same multi-folder group = guaranteed second project. (No main workspace → sidebar row via `ensureProjectInSidebar` only, project.ts / handlers.ts:153-160.)
- Second renderer path `useEnsureV2Project.ts:31-62` has the same shape: `findByPath` → fall through to `create` on zero candidates.

## 3. How reinstall surfaces/creates the duplicate

Mechanism A — duplicate row minted at re-import: if the host `projects.repoPath` row is missing or mismatching (host.db wiped/moved, org id changed — the DB is per-org `~/.superset/host/<orgId>/host.db`; OR case-only folder rename breaking the BINARY compare) AND the repo has no parseable GitHub remote, `findByPath` returns 0 candidates → renderer creates a brand-new project named after the folder basename's CURRENT casing. Old cloud row "PX V1 apps" (slug `px-v1-apps`) persists; new one becomes "PX v1 apps" (slug `px-v1-apps-2` via the retry loop). Both innerJoin-render once each has a `v2SidebarProjects` row.

Mechanism B — pre-existing duplicate resurrected by the v1.14 auto-placement change: `usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar` (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/components/AgentHooks/hooks/usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar/usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar.ts:51-63` + `selectWorktreesToPlace.ts:18-28`) runs on startup and, for every LOCAL `worktree`-type workspace with no `v2WorkspaceLocalState` row, calls `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar(worktree.id, worktree.projectId)` — which INSERTS a `v2SidebarProjects` row for that projectId (`useDashboardSidebarState.ts:226-232`). In the v1.14.0 merge (commit `1e89c577b` "offline-first host-owned workspace table", contained in `2b0a3ef44`/`e376fbf79`), this hook's source switched from the cloud `v2Workspaces` Electric collection to `useHostWorkspaces()` (host.db, host-owned). So after installing the v1.14.2 build, any worktree rows sitting in `~/.superset/host/<org>/host.db` under an old duplicate projectId — invisible before — get auto-placed and the duplicate project row appears in the sidebar without any user action. If the renderer localStorage was reset (fresh `%APPDATA%` profile / uninstall with data removal), ALL placement tombstones are gone and every host worktree re-places, resurrecting every project that has a worktree, including stale duplicates.

Name-from-basename confirmation: yes — `useFolderFirstImport.ts:66,116` uses `getBaseName(repoPath)`; folder-name case directly becomes the project name; slug lowercases it so case-variants collide on slug and trigger the suffix-retry duplicate instead of a reuse or a loud error.

## 4. Upstream v1.14.x changes

- `git diff da244dbca(v1.13) e376fbf79(v1.14.2)`: NO change to the dedupe keys — `findByPath`, `createCloudProjectWithSlugRetry`, `v2Project.create/findByGitHubRemote` all unchanged.
- The relevant change is `1e89c577b` (#5452, in the v1.14.0 merge): workspaces became HOST-OWNED; `usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar` switched from cloud `v2Workspaces` to `useHostWorkspaces` (diff shown above), and cloud `v2Workspaces` became a read-only R2 fallback (`collections.ts:484-503` comment). This changed which workspaces are auto-placed (host.db truth) — the vector by which a latent duplicate becomes visible right after installing the new build.
- `project.remove` in the same window only changed local-row deletion to per-row broadcast (project.ts:765-773).

## 5. Zero-child project rows

Every `v2SidebarProjects` row that joins a `v2Projects` row is ALWAYS rendered — no hide-on-empty path. `computedGroups` in `useDashboardSidebarData.ts:777-866` unconditionally `return [sidebarProject]` for each membership row regardless of `children.length`. Children require: (a) a `v2WorkspaceLocalState` row whose workspaceId resolves in `useHostWorkspaces` (lines 265-288 — a local-state row whose workspace is missing from the host yields nothing), or (b) the auto-included local MAIN workspace, gated on `hostId === machineId` AND project already in sidebar (`sidebarVisibility.ts:33-50`, used at `useDashboardSidebarData.ts:492-511`). So a duplicate project whose workspaces are absent from this host's host.db (or whose main workspace is on another/old host id) renders as a bare project row with no visible children — exactly the reported "PX v1 apps" row. The count badge is `flattenedCollapsedWorkspaces.length` over `project.children` (`DashboardSidebarProjectSection.tsx:39-42,73`, rendered at `DashboardSidebarProjectRow.tsx:122`) — a "1" with no visible children is consistent with a collapsed row or a child inside a section.

## Bottom line

One local repo maps to N cloud `v2_projects` rows by design; dedupe rests on exactly two keys — case-sensitive `projects.repoPath` equality in the per-org host sqlite, and case-insensitive GitHub `repoCloneUrl` match (only when a GitHub remote exists). Miss both (renamed-case folder, wiped/per-org-switched host.db, no GitHub remote, or any multi-repo re-create) and `project.create` mints a new project whose case-lowered slug collides — the slug-retry loop (handlers.ts:74-107) resolves the collision by suffixing rather than reusing, permanently creating "PX V1 apps" (px-v1-apps) + "PX v1 apps" (px-v1-apps-2). The v1.14.2 switch of startup worktree auto-placement to host-owned workspace data (`usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar` + `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar` inserting `v2SidebarProjects` rows) is what makes both rows appear in the sidebar right after the reinstall. Fix directions: case-insensitive repoPath compare (lower/`COLLATE NOCASE`) in `findByPath`, and treating a slug conflict on create as "candidate exists — ask/reuse" instead of suffix-retry.",

== 3. Archive classification for mains (getWorkspaceSidebarBucket) ==
File: apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/providers/CollectionsProvider/dashboardSidebarLocal/sidebarVisibility.ts
- Bucket order (:146-175): deleted (deletedAt, checked FIRST) → completed → archived → snoozed → hidden (raw isHidden) → active.
- isWorkspaceArchived (:114-125): archived if `archivedAt != null`, OR `isHidden === true && type !== "main"`. So: a removed NON-main with bare isHidden surfaces as Archived; a main with bare isHidden buckets as "hidden" (invisible everywhere) — that combination is now LEGACY only (pre MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY removes, and whole-project teardown; comment :55-61). - archiveWorkspace (useDashboardSidebarState.ts:524-565): for an auto-included main with NO local-state row, INSERTS a pre-archived row (isHidden:true + archivedAt) — ":530 a master card can be archived but never hard-removed".
- ensureSidebarWorkspaceRecord (useDashboardSidebarState.ts:143-192): on explicit open, ONLY a "hidden"-bucket row (legacy removed main) is pulled back to active; archived/snoozed/deleted rows stay put (view-in-place).
- Mains never reach "deleted" bucket: only setters of workspace deletedAt are `deleteWorkspace` (guarded) and kanban deleteCard→deleteWorkspace (same guard).

File: `C:\Users\khair\.superset\worktrees\d7ac4c2f-2b74-4e23-a35c-f6c443cf7cf5\muddled-cake\apps\desktop\src\renderer\routes\_authenticated\providers\CollectionsProvider\collections.ts`

- `v2Projects` — CLOUD-synced Electric shape on Postgres table `v2_projects` (lines 349-380), wrapped in `createPersistedElectricCollection` → persisted to a renderer-side SQLite cache via `createElectronSQLitePersistence` (lines 100-102, 116-128). Cloud DB is source of truth; rows survive any reinstall.
- `v2SidebarProjects` — DEVICE-LOCAL `localStorageCollectionOptions`, storageKey `v2-sidebar-projects-${organizationId}`, key = `item.projectId` (lines 784-791). Lives in the renderer's `window.localStorage` → Electron userData dir (`%APPDATA%\Superset\...\Local Storage`). NOT cloud-synced.
- `v2WorkspaceLocalState` (per-workspace sidebar placement) — also localStorage, storageKey `v2-workspace-local-state-${organizationId}` (lines 797-811).
- Third store: host-service local SQLite `host.db` at `~/.superset/host/<organizationId>/host.db` (`apps/desktop/src/main/lib/host-service-coordinator.ts:482`, `host-service-manifest.ts:19-21`, `app-environment.ts:8-9`). Its `projects` table (`packages/host-service/src/db/schema.ts:57`) is the ONLY place `repoPath` is stored — the cloud `v2_projects` table has NO path column (`packages/db/src/schema/schema.ts:402-429`).
- NSIS installer (`apps/desktop/electron-builder.ts:185-193`) does NOT set `deleteAppDataOnUninstall`; both `%APPDATA%` and `~/.superset` normally survive reinstall.

Sidebar render = `v2SidebarProjects` innerJoin `v2Projects` (`useDashboardSidebarData.ts:157-192`). A project row renders iff a localStorage membership row exists AND the cloud project row exists.

## 2. Creation flow + the dedupe key at each site

Renderer folder import: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/AddRepositoryModals/hooks/useFolderFirstImport/useFolderFirstImport.ts`
- line 58: `client.project.findByPath.query({ repoPath })` — the ONLY dedupe probe.
- lines 98-107: if exactly one candidate → `project.setup` (reuse).
- lines 115-120: if ZERO candidates → `project.create` with `name: getBaseName(repoPath)` (line 66/116) — **project name = folder basename, case-preserved from the OS picker path** (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/lib/pathBasename/pathBasename.ts:1-3`).
- After create/setup: `useFinalizeProjectSetup.ts:22-32` → `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar`/`ensureProjectInSidebar` → `ensureSidebarProjectRecord` inserts the `v2SidebarProjects` row (`useDashboardSidebarState.ts:125-141, 219-232`).

Host-service dedupe probe `project.findByPath` (`packages/host-service/src/trpc/router/project/project.ts:154-412`):
- **Key 1 (local, folder-first default)**: exact string equality on the host's local sqlite — `ctx.db.query.projects.findFirst({ where: eq(projects.repoPath, gitRoot) })` (lines 231-233; non-git variant line 183-185). SQLite TEXT with default BINARY collation → **CASE-SENSITIVE full-path compare**. `gitRoot` comes from `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` (`utils/resolve-repo.ts:143-151, 169-183`), i.e. current on-disk casing. A case-only folder rename (PX V1 apps → PX v1 apps) makes the stored row unfindable.
- **Key 2 (cloud fallback)**: only if the repo has a **parseable GitHub remote** — `v2Project.findByGitHubRemote` compares `lower(repoCloneUrl)` case-insensitively (`packages/trpc/src/router/v2-project/v2-project.ts:126-167`, esp. 140-144, 161). Repo with no remote, or non-GitHub remote (e.g. an Anvil remote) → `if (!parsed) return { candidates: [] }` (project.ts:254-255) → **no cloud dedupe at all**.
- **Name and slug are NEVER used as a dedupe key.**

Host-service create (`packages/host-service/src/trpc/router/project/handlers.ts`):
- `createFromImportLocal` (239-254) / `createFromNonGitFolder` (263-275) → `persistFromResolved` (120-203): inserts local `projects` row keyed by fresh `randomUUID()` (line 138) with `repoPath` (`utils/persist-project.ts:5-23`), then cloud `v2Project.create`.
- `createCloudProjectWithSlugRetry` (74-107): slug = `slugifyProjectName(name)` — **lowercased** basename (lines 26-39, so "PX V1 apps" and "PX v1 apps" both → `px-v1-apps`). On the `v2_projects_org_slug_unique` CONFLICT ("Project slug already exists", v2-project.ts:261-267) it does **NOT reuse the existing project — it retries with `px-v1-apps-2`, `-3` … up to 100** (lines 81-101) and silently mints a SECOND cloud project row. This is THE duplicate-manufacturing site.
- Cloud `v2Project.create` itself (`v2-project.ts:169-317`) is a dumb insert; DB uniqueness is only `unique("v2_projects_org_slug_unique").on(organizationId, slug)` (`packages/db/src/schema/schema.ts:427`) — no uniqueness on name or repoCloneUrl ("Multiple v2 projects may point at the same GitHub URL" is explicitly allowed, project.ts:148-151).
- `createFromMultiRepo` (288-366): **zero dedupe of any kind** — always `randomUUID()` + fresh anchor dir `~/.superset/multi-repo/<projectId>`; its anchor `repoPath` never matches any member repo path, so `findByPath` on a member can never find it. Re-creating the same multi-folder group = guaranteed second project. (No main workspace → sidebar row via `ensureProjectInSidebar` only, project.ts / handlers.ts:153-160.)
- Second renderer path `useEnsureV2Project.ts:31-62` has the same shape: `findByPath` → fall through to `create` on zero candidates.

## 3. How reinstall surfaces/creates the duplicate

Mechanism A — duplicate row minted at re-import: if the host `projects.repoPath` row is missing or mismatching (host.db wiped/moved, org id changed — the DB is per-org `~/.superset/host/<orgId>/host.db`; OR case-only folder rename breaking the BINARY compare) AND the repo has no parseable GitHub remote, `findByPath` returns 0 candidates → renderer creates a brand-new project named after the folder basename's CURRENT casing. Old cloud row "PX V1 apps" (slug `px-v1-apps`) persists; new one becomes "PX v1 apps" (slug `px-v1-apps-2` via the retry loop). Both innerJoin-render once each has a `v2SidebarProjects` row.

Mechanism B — pre-existing duplicate resurrected by the v1.14 auto-placement change: `usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar` (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/components/AgentHooks/hooks/usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar/usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar.ts:51-63` + `selectWorktreesToPlace.ts:18-28`) runs on startup and, for every LOCAL `worktree`-type workspace with no `v2WorkspaceLocalState` row, calls `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar(worktree.id, worktree.projectId)` — which INSERTS a `v2SidebarProjects` row for that projectId (`useDashboardSidebarState.ts:226-232`). In the v1.14.0 merge (commit `1e89c577b` "offline-first host-owned workspace table", contained in `2b0a3ef44`/`e376fbf79`), this hook's source switched from the cloud `v2Workspaces` Electric collection to `useHostWorkspaces()` (host.db, host-owned). So after installing the v1.14.2 build, any worktree rows sitting in `~/.superset/host/<org>/host.db` under an old duplicate projectId — invisible before — get auto-placed and the duplicate project row appears in the sidebar without any user action. If the renderer localStorage was reset (fresh `%APPDATA%` profile / uninstall with data removal), ALL placement tombstones are gone and every host worktree re-places, resurrecting every project that has a worktree, including stale duplicates.

Name-from-basename confirmation: yes — `useFolderFirstImport.ts:66,116` uses `getBaseName(repoPath)`; folder-name case directly becomes the project name; slug lowercases it so case-variants collide on slug and trigger the suffix-retry duplicate instead of a reuse or a loud error.

Upstream v1.14.x changes\n\n- `git diff da244dbca(v1.13) e376fbf79(v1.14.2)`: NO change to the dedupe keys — `findByPath`, `createCloudProjectWithSlugRetry`, `v2Project.create/findByGitHubRemote` all unchanged.\n- The relevant change is `1e89c577b` (#5452, in the v1.14.0 merge): workspaces became HOST-OWNED; `usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar` switched from cloud `v2Workspaces` to `useHostWorkspaces` (diff shown above), and cloud `v2Workspaces` became a read-only R2 fallback (`collections.ts:484-503` comment). This changed which workspaces are auto-placed (host.db truth) — the vector by which a latent duplicate becomes visible right after installing the new build.\n- `project.remove` in the same window only changed local-row deletion to per-row broadcast (project.ts:765-773).\n\n## 5. Zero-child project rows\n\nEvery `v2SidebarProjects` row that joins a `v2Projects` row is ALWAYS rendered — no hide-on-empty path. `computedGroups` in `useDashboardSidebarData.ts:777-866` unconditionally `return [sidebarProject]` for each membership row regardless of `children.length`. Children require: (a) a `v2WorkspaceLocalState` row whose workspaceId resolves in `useHostWorkspaces` (lines 265-288 — a local-state row whose workspace is missing from the host yields nothing), or (b) the auto-included local MAIN workspace, gated on `hostId === machineId` AND project already in sidebar (`sidebarVisibility.ts:33-50`, used at `useDashboardSidebarData.ts:492-511`). So a duplicate project whose workspaces are absent from this host's host.db (or whose main workspace is on another/old host id) renders as a bare project row with no visible children — exactly the reported \"PX v1 apps\" row. The count badge is `flattenedCollapsedWorkspaces.length` over `project.children` (`DashboardSidebarProjectSection.tsx:39-42,73`, rendered at `DashboardSidebarProjectRow.tsx:122`) — a \"1\" with no visible children is consistent with a collapsed row or a child inside a section.\n\n## Bottom line\n\nOne local repo maps to N cloud `v2_projects` rows by design; dedupe rests on exactly two keys — case-sensitive `projects.repoPath` equality in the per-org host sqlite, and case-insensitive GitHub `repoCloneUrl` match (only when a GitHub remote exists). Miss both (renamed-case folder, wiped/per-org-switched host.db, no GitHub remote, or any multi-repo re-create) and `project.create` mints a new project whose case-lowered slug collides — the slug-retry loop (handlers.ts:74-107) resolves the collision by suffixing rather than reusing, permanently creating \"PX V1 apps\" (px-v1-apps) + \"PX v1 apps\" (px-v1-apps-2). The v1.14.2 switch of startup worktree auto-placement to host-owned workspace data (`usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar` + `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar` inserting `v2SidebarProjects` rows) is what makes both rows appear in the sidebar right after the reinstall. ## 5. Zero-child project rows

Children require: (a) a `v2WorkspaceLocalState` row whose workspaceId resolves in `useHostWorkspaces` (lines 265-288 — a local-state row whose workspace is missing from the host yields nothing), or (b) the auto-included local MAIN workspace, gated on `hostId === machineId` AND project already in sidebar (`sidebarVisibility.ts:33-50`, used at `useDashboardSidebarData.ts:492-511`). So a duplicate project whose workspaces are absent from this host's host.db (or whose main workspace is on another/old host id) renders as a bare project row with no visible children — exactly the reported \"PX v1 apps\" row. The count badge is `flattenedCollapsedWorkspaces.length` over `project.children` (`DashboardSidebarProjectSection.tsx:39-42,73`, rendered at `DashboardSidebarProjectRow.tsx:122`) — a \"1\" with no visible children is consistent with a collapsed row or a child inside a section.

## Bottom line

One local repo maps to N cloud `v2_projects` rows by design; dedupe rests on exactly two keys — case-sensitive `projects.repoPath` equality in the per-org host sqlite, and case-insensitive GitHub `repoCloneUrl` match (only when a GitHub remote exists). Miss both (renamed-case folder, wiped/per-org-switched host.db, no GitHub remote, or any multi-repo re-create) and `project.create` mints a new project whose case-lowered slug collides — the slug-retry loop (handlers.ts:74-107) resolves the collision by suffixing rather than reusing, permanently creating \"PX V1 apps\" (px-v1-apps) + \"PX v1 apps\" (px-v1-apps-2). The v1.14.2 switch of startup worktree auto-placement to host-owned workspace data (`usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar` + `ensureWorkspaceInSidebar` inserting `v2SidebarProjects` rows) is what makes both rows appear in the sidebar right after the reinstall. Fix directions: case-insensitive repoPath compare (lower/`COLLATE NOCASE`) in `findByPath`, and treating a slug conflict on create as \"candidate exists — ask/reuse\" instead of suffix-retry.",

apps\desktop\src\renderer\routes\_authenticated\components\AgentHooks\hooks\usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar\index.ts
apps\desktop\src\renderer\routes\_authenticated\components\AgentHooks\hooks\usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar\selectWorktreesToPlace.test.ts
apps\desktop\src\renderer\routes\_authenticated\components\AgentHooks\hooks\usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar\selectWorktreesToPlace.ts
apps\desktop\src\renderer\routes\_authenticated\components\AgentHooks\hooks\usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar\usePlaceLocalWorktreesInSidebar.ts

1	export type LocalWorkspaceForPlacement = {
2		id: string;
3		projectId: string;
4		type: "main" | "worktree";
5	};
6	
7	/**
8	 * Chooses which of this device's workspaces the sidebar reconciler should
9	 * place. Kept free of React so it can be unit-tested directly.
10	 *
11	 * Only `worktree` workspaces are eligible: the host creates a `main` for every
12	 * project on the device, so placing those would drag locally-known projects the
13	 * user never added into the sidebar. Main workspaces surface instead via the
14	 * gated `isAutoIncludedLocalMainWorkspace` path. A workspace that already has a
15	 * local-state row is "already placed" and skipped, so nothing the user has
16	 * moved, hidden, or removed is re-added.
17	 */
18	export function selectWorktreesToPlace(
19		localWorkspaces: readonly LocalWorkspaceForPlacement[],
20		placedWorkspaceIds: ReadonlySet<string>,
21	): Array<{ id: string; projectId: string }> {
22		return localWorkspaces
23			.filter(
24				(workspace) =>
25					workspace.type === "worktree" && !placedWorkspaceIds.has(workspace.id),
26			)
27			.map((workspace) => ({ id: workspace.id, projectId: workspace.projectId }));
28	}
29
