content.text: Root causes nailed. Asking decisions before implementing.

questions.question: Duplicate explained: they are TWO real projects in cloud DB. "PX V1 apps" (created 9 Jun) = your multi-repo project (8 repos, 23 branch worktrees). "PX v1 apps" (created 29 May) = the OLD plain-folder project pointing at C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\PX v1 apps (superseded by the multi-repo one). Its sidebar row was re-inserted TODAY 18:05 — something opened/ensured that workspace (not automatic on launch; v1-import modal is manual-only). What do you want done with the old "PX v1 apps" row?

options.label: You remove it (2 clicks) (Recommended)
options.description: Right-click the lower "PX v1 apps" row → Remove from Sidebar. No data loss — folder + project stay, re-addable from the Workspaces page. Nothing automatic re-adds main-only projects, so it sticks.

options.label: Remove + purge project entirely
options.description: Also delete the old project record from cloud DB + host registry so it never shows in the Workspaces page either. I'd wire/execute the host-side project removal. Folder on disk untouched.

options.label: Keep both
options.description: Leave as-is; they're legitimately two separate projects.

questions.question: Snooze root cause is a v1.14.2 upstream-merge regression: the sidebar row projection dropped snoozeUntil/archivedAt/completedAt/deletedAt fields, so snoozing WRITES but the row never leaves the active lane (Snoozed always 0, Recycle Bin always 0, archived masters vanish, completed cards misfile under Archived). I'm fixing that. Separate question: master (main) rows — snooze currently also silently no-ops for them due to a missing insert-fallback. Should master rows be snoozeable?

options.label: Yes, snoozeable (Recommended)
options.description: Fix the no-op: snooze works on every row incl. master. Master stays never-deletable; snooze is temporary hide with auto-return.

options.label: No, hide Snooze on masters
options.description: Sidebar matches kanban: masters get Archive only; Snooze menu hidden for master rows.

questions.question: "Master never deleted, at worst archived" is ALREADY enforced (fork marker MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY): every delete entry point hides/refuses masters + host-service hard-rejects destroy. I found 3 residual gaps and will harden them: (1) missing-worktree error page still shows a Delete button for masters (click silently no-ops) → becomes Archive; (2) the renderer delete guard can fail open if the workspace-type cache isn't hydrated → will fail loud instead; (3) Empty Recycle Bin loop gets a defensive master skip. Also found a latent Windows footgun: project dedupe compares repo paths CASE-SENSITIVELY, so a case-only folder rename mints a duplicate project (slug-suffix -2) instead of reusing. Include that hardening too?

options.label: All 3 gaps + path dedupe fix (Recommended)
options.description: Case-insensitive repoPath match in host-service findByPath (Windows paths are case-insensitive). Prevents future duplicate-project minting.

options.label: 3 gaps only
options.description: Skip the host-service dedupe change; renderer-only hardening this batch.

answers.Duplicate explained: they are TWO real p: When I'm trying to remove the project I don't want it to come back, so how can I make sure it stays away? I did this last time, and now it's come back this time.

content: We need to be able to cover this next time. I don't want there to be any automatic, straight rebase. Any time we rebase from upstream, we need to ensure it goes through AI review gate so that it doesn't break any of our custom features.

1	name: Nightly upstream merge
2	
3	# The ongoing half of the architecture. Nightly: if upstream superset published a
4	# newer desktop-v* tag than .fork/upstream-baseline.txt, merge that tag into the
5	# fork. git's 3-way merge handles the non-conflicting majority deterministically;
6	# Opus (--effort high) resolves ONLY the conflicted files, preserving every
7	# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the deterministic ARM64
8	# build. On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
9	# fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
10	# build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline untouched -> recovery loop.
11	#
12	# AI cost scales with the UPSTREAM delta per release, NOT with how large the fork
13	# grows. The build itself stays 100% AI-free.
14	#
15	# NOTE: schedule only fires from the DEFAULT branch, so this is dormant until the
16	# fork-baseline branch is promoted to main.
17	
18	on:
19	  schedule:
20	    - cron: '0 5 * * *' # 05:00 UTC daily
21	  workflow_dispatch:
22	    inputs:
23	      force_tag:
24	        description: 'Merge a specific upstream desktop-v* tag (blank = latest)'
25	        required: false
26	        default: ''
27	
28	permissions:
29	  contents: write
30	
31	jobs:
32	  merge:
33	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
34	    outputs:
35	      should_build: ${{ steps.do.outputs.should_build }}
36	      newtag: ${{ steps.do.outputs.newtag }}
37	      version: ${{ steps.do.outputs.version }}
38	      candidate: ${{ steps.do.outputs.candidate }}
39	    env:
40	      CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
41	      DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS: '1'
42	      CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC: '1'
43	      # Conflict resolution can touch large files; give Opus headroom.
44	      CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS: '128000'
45	    steps:
46	      - name: Checkout fork (full history)
47	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
48	        with:
49	          fetch-depth: 0
50	
51	      - name: Setup Node.js
52	        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
53	        with:
54	          node-version: '22'
55	
56	      # (LOCK-REGEN) Lock regeneration below needs bun; pin the EXACT version
57	      # the build uses so the lock we commit is byte-identical to what the
58	      # frozen build install consumes.
59	      - name: Setup Bun (match the build's pinned version)
60	        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
61	        with:
62	          bun-version: '1.3.14'
63	
64	      - name: Merge upstream + resolve + gate
65	        id: do
66	        shell: bash
67	        run: |
68	          set -euo pipefail
69	          git config user.name "superset-arm64-bot"
70	          git config user.email "actions@users.noreply.github.com"
71	
72	          # --- detect the upstream tag to merge --------------------------------
73	          git remote add upstream https://github.com/superset-sh/superset.git 2>/dev/null || true
74	          # Fetch upstream's desktop-v* tags into an ISOLATED ref namespace.
75	          # Our OWN one-version release tags live in refs/tags/desktop-v<version>
76	          # (gh release create makes them at our build commit). A plain fetch into
77	          # refs/tags/ collides with every released version ("would clobber existing
78	          # tag" -> non-zero fetch -> set -e abort). The isolated namespace + --force
79	          # keeps upstream's source tags cleanly separate from our release tags.
80	          git fetch --no-recurse-submodules --force upstream \
81	            'refs/tags/desktop-v*:refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*'
82	          FORCE='${{ github.event.inputs.force_tag }}'
83	          if [ -n "$FORCE" ]; then
84	            NEWTAG="$FORCE"
85	          else
86	            LATEST=$(git for-each-ref --format='%(refname:lstrip=2)' 'refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*' | sed -n 's/^desktop-v\([0-9][0-9.]*\)$/\1/p' | sort -V | tail -1)
87	            NEWTAG="desktop-v${LATEST}"
88	          fi
89	          BASE=$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .fork/upstream-baseline.txt)
90	          echo "baseline=$BASE  latest_upstream=$NEWTAG"
91	          if [ "$NEWTAG" = "$BASE" ]; then
92	            echo "Already at $BASE — nothing to merge."
93	            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
94	            exit 0
95	          fi
96	          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
97	            echo "::error::upstream tag $NEWTAG not found"; exit 1
98	          fi
99	
100	          # --- candidate branch + merge ---------------------------------------
101	          CAND="nightly/${NEWTAG}"
102	          git switch -c "$CAND"
103	          echo "Merging $NEWTAG into the fork (no auto-commit)…"
104	          set +e
105	          git merge --no-commit --no-ff "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}"
106	          set -e
107	          CONFLICTS=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
108	          if [ -n "$CONFLICTS" ]; then
109	            echo "::group::conflicts to resolve"; echo "$CONFLICTS"; echo "::endgroup::"
110	            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
111	            PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository mid-merge: upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} is being merged into our Windows-ARM64 fork. ONLY the files listed by 'git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U' are conflicted. Resolve EVERY conflicted file: combine upstream's change with our fork's feature so BOTH intents survive and our feature keeps working; where upstream deliberately removed or refactored something we had only lightly touched, follow upstream. Do NOT edit any non-conflicted file. Do NOT weaken or remove any feature marker tracked in FEATURES.md. After resolving each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Do NOT run any other git command. When every conflict is resolved and staged, stop. Output nothing."
112	            attempt=1
113	            while [ $attempt -le 3 ]; do
114	              echo "::group::claude resolve attempt $attempt"
115	              claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PROMPT" || true
116	              echo "::endgroup::"
117	              if [ -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)" ]; then break; fi
118	              attempt=$((attempt+1))
119	            done
120	            REMAIN=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
121	            if [ -n "$REMAIN" ]; then
122	              echo "::error::Opus could not resolve all conflicts for $NEWTAG — recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Remaining:"; echo "$REMAIN"
123	              exit 1
124	            fi
125	          else
126	            echo "Clean merge — no conflicts."
127	          fi
128	
129	          # --- feature-marker gate (FEATURES.md) ------------------------------
130	          echo "::group::feature-marker gate"
131	          MISSING=0
132	          # read the fenced ```markers block: lines are "<token><TAB><glob-root>"
133	          awk '/^```markers$/{f=1;next} /^```$/{f=0} f' FEATURES.md | while IFS=$'\t' read -r tok root; do
134	            [ -n "$tok" ] || continue
135	            if grep -rqsF -- "$tok" "$root" 2>/dev/null; then
136	              echo "  OK   $tok ($root)"
137	            else
138	              echo "::error::feature marker MISSING after merge: '$tok' under $root"
139	              echo missing >> "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing"
140	            fi
141	          done
142	          echo "::endgroup::"
143	          if [ -f "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing" ]; then
144	            echo "::error::A feature was dropped by the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
145	            exit 1
146	          fi
147	
148	          # --- dependency-consistency gate + lock regen (LOCK-REGEN) ----------
149	          # The build installs --frozen-lockfile and electron-builder's
150	          # dependency traversal requires each workspace's DECLARED dep version
151	          # to be the one actually installed. A merge introduces two kinds of
152	          # drift that both kill the build ~25 min in (after a windows-arm64
153	          # runner spins up), not here:
154	          #   (1) a fork root `overrides` entry pins a package to a version
155	          #       DIFFERENT from the merged workspace pin (this is exactly how a
156	          #       stale {@mastra/core, mastracode} override broke every mastra
157	          #       bump: override forced 0.18.0, chat declared 0.18.1 ->
158	          #       electron-builder "production dependency not found 0.18.1"); and
159	          #   (2) the merge bumps a package.json dep but bun.lock still pins the
160	          #       old version (a stale frozen lock).
161	          # Catch (1) loud here (pure manifest analysis, platform-independent);
162	          # fix (2) by regenerating the lock with the build's exact bun version,
163	          # which ALSO fails loud if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin
164	          # is age-blocked. Both run in this 2-min job, so the windows-arm64
165	          # build never spins up on a broken tree. We deliberately do NOT run a
166	          # full `--frozen-lockfile` install here: this is a linux runner, so a
167	          # platform-specific install/native-build quirk would be a false abort,
168	          # and after a successful --lockfile-only the lock is consistent with
169	          # the manifests by construction (same bun version the build uses).
170	          # Any failure below hard-aborts with the baseline untouched.
171	          echo "::group::dependency-consistency gate"
172	          node scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs
173	          echo "::endgroup::"
174	
175	          echo "::group::regenerate bun.lock to match merged manifests"
176	          export SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1
177	          LOCKLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/bun-lockfile.log"
178	          set +e
179	          bun install --lockfile-only > "$LOCKLOG" 2>&1
180	          rc=$?
181	          set -e
182	          cat "$LOCKLOG"
183	          if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
184	            if grep -q "blocked by minimum-release-age" "$LOCKLOG"; then
185	              echo "::error::Lock regen blocked by bunfig minimumReleaseAge — an upstream dep pin in $NEWTAG is <72h old. This is a timing race, not a breakage: the nightly will succeed once the version ages past the gate (re-run after it ages, or wait for tomorrow's schedule). Baseline left untouched."
186	            else
187	              echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
188	            fi
189	            exit 1
190	          fi
191	          echo "::endgroup::"
192	
193	          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
194	          printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
195	          git add -A
196	          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present."
197	          # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
198	          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
199	          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
200	          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
201	          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
202	          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
203	          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
204	          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
205	          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
206	          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
207	          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
208	          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
209	          git push origin "$CAND"
210	
211	          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
212	          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
213	          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
214	          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
215	          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
216	          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND (version $VERSION). Building next."
217	
218	  build:
219	    needs: merge
220	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
221	    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
222	    with:
223	      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}
224	      publish: true
225	
226	  advance:
227	    needs: [merge, build]
228	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
229	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
230	    steps:
231	      - name: Checkout
232	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
233	        with:
234	          fetch-depth: 0
235	      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
236	        shell: bash
237	        run: |
238	          set -euo pipefail
239	          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
240	          git fetch origin "$CAND"
241	          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
242	          git push origin FETCH_HEAD:refs/heads/main
243	          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
244	          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."
245

file.content:
name: Nightly upstream merge

# The ongoing half of the architecture. Nightly: if upstream superset published a
# newer desktop-v* tag than .fork/upstream-baseline.txt, merge that tag into the
# fork. git's 3-way merge handles the non-conflicting majority deterministically;
# Opus (--effort high) resolves ONLY the conflicted files, preserving every
# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the deterministic ARM64
# build. On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
# fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
# build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline untouched -> recovery loop.
#
# AI cost scales with the UPSTREAM delta per release, NOT with how large the fork
# grows. The build itself stays 100% AI-free.
#
# NOTE: schedule only fires from the DEFAULT branch, so this is dormant until the
# fork-baseline branch is promoted to main.

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 5 * * *' # 05:00 UTC daily
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      force_tag:
        description: 'Merge a specific upstream desktop-v* tag (blank = latest)'
        required: false
        default: ''

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  merge:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      should_build: ${{ steps.do.outputs.should_build }}
      newtag: ${{ steps.do.outputs.newtag }}
      version: ${{ steps.do.outputs.version }}
      candidate: ${{ steps.do.outputs.candidate }}
    env:
      CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS: '1'
      CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC: '1'
      # Conflict resolution can touch large files; give Opus headroom.
      CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS: '128000'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout fork (full history)
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'

      # (LOCK-REGEN) Lock regeneration below needs bun; pin the EXACT version
      # the build uses so the lock we commit is byte-identical to what the
      # frozen build install consumes.
      - name: Setup Bun (match the build's pinned version)
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: '1.3.14'

      - name: Merge upstream + resolve + gate
        id: do
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git config user.name "superset-arm64-bot"
          git config user.email "actions@users.noreply.github.com"

          # --- detect the upstream tag to merge --------------------------------
          git remote add upstream https://github.com/superset-sh/superset.git 2>/dev/null || true
          # Fetch upstream's desktop-v* tags into an ISOLATED ref namespace.
          # Our OWN one-version release tags live in refs/tags/desktop-v<version>
          # (gh release create makes them at our build commit). A plain fetch into
          # refs/tags/ collides with every released version ("would clobber existing
          # tag" -> non-zero fetch -> set -e abort). The isolated namespace + --force
          # keeps upstream's source tags cleanly separate from our release tags.
          git fetch --no-recurse-submodules --force upstream \
            'refs/tags/desktop-v*:refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*'
          FORCE='${{ github.event.inputs.force_tag }}'
          if [ -n "$FORCE" ]; then
            NEWTAG="$FORCE"
          else
            LATEST=$(git for-each-ref --format='%(refname:lstrip=2)' 'refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*' | sed -n 's/^desktop-v\([0-9][0-9.]*\)$/\1/p' | sort -V | tail -1)
            NEWTAG="desktop-v${LATEST}"
          fi
          BASE=$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .fork/upstream-baseline.txt)
          echo "baseline=$BASE  latest_upstream=$NEWTAG"
          if [ "$NEWTAG" = "$BASE" ]; then
            echo "Already at $BASE — nothing to merge."
            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            exit 0
          fi
          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::upstream tag $NEWTAG not found"; exit 1
          fi

          # --- candidate branch + merge ---------------------------------------
          CAND="nightly/${NEWTAG}"
          git switch -c "$CAND"
          echo "Merging $NEWTAG into the fork (no auto-commit)…"
          set +e
          git merge --no-commit --no-ff "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}"
          set -e
          CONFLICTS=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
          if [ -n "$CONFLICTS" ]; then
            echo "::group::conflicts to resolve"; echo "$CONFLICTS"; echo "::endgroup::"
            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
            PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository mid-merge: upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} is being merged into our Windows-ARM64 fork. ONLY the files listed by 'git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U' are conflicted. Resolve EVERY conflicted file: combine upstream's change with our fork's feature so BOTH intents survive and our feature keeps working; where upstream deliberately removed or refactored something we had only lightly touched, follow upstream. Do NOT edit any non-conflicted file. Do NOT weaken or remove any feature marker tracked in FEATURES.md. After resolving each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Do NOT run any other git command. When every conflict is resolved and staged, stop. Output nothing."
            attempt=1
            while [ $attempt -le 3 ]; do
              echo "::group::claude resolve attempt $attempt"
              claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PROMPT" || true
              echo "::endgroup::"
              if [ -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)" ]; then break; fi
              attempt=$((attempt+1))
            done
            REMAIN=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
            if [ -n "$REMAIN" ]; then
              echo "::error::Opus could not resolve all conflicts for $NEWTAG — recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Remaining:"; echo "$REMAIN"
              exit 1
            fi
          else
            echo "Clean merge — no conflicts."
          fi

          # --- feature-marker gate (FEATURES.md) ------------------------------
          echo "::group::feature-marker gate"
          MISSING=0
          # read the fenced ```markers block: lines are "<token><TAB><glob-root>"
          awk '/^```markers$/{f=1;next} /^```$/{f=0} f' FEATURES.md | while IFS=$'\t' read -r tok root; do
            [ -n "$tok" ] || continue
            if grep -rqsF -- "$tok" "$root" 2>/dev/null; then
              echo "  OK   $tok ($root)"
            else
              echo "::error::feature marker MISSING after merge: '$tok' under $root"
              echo missing >> "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing"
            fi
          done
          echo "::endgroup::"
          if [ -f "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing" ]; then
            echo "::error::A feature was dropped by the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi

          # --- dependency-consistency gate + lock regen (LOCK-REGEN) ----------
          # The build installs --frozen-lockfile and electron-builder's
          # dependency traversal requires each workspace's DECLARED dep version
          # to be the one actually installed. A merge introduces two kinds of
          # drift that both kill the build ~25 min in (after a windows-arm64
          # runner spins up), not here:
          #   (1) a fork root `overrides` entry pins a package to a version
          #       DIFFERENT from the merged workspace pin (this is exactly how a
          #       stale {@mastra/core, mastracode} override broke every mastra
          #       bump: override forced 0.18.0, chat declared 0.18.1 ->
          #       electron-builder "production dependency not found 0.18.1"); and
          #   (2) the merge bumps a package.json dep but bun.lock still pins the
          #       old version (a stale frozen lock).
          # Catch (1) loud here (pure manifest analysis, platform-independent);
          # fix (2) by regenerating the lock with the build's exact bun version,
          # which ALSO fails loud if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin
          # is age-blocked. Both run in this 2-min job, so the windows-arm64
          # build never spins up on a broken tree. We deliberately do NOT run a
          # full `--frozen-lockfile` install here: this is a linux runner, so a
          # platform-specific install/native-build quirk would be a false abort,
          # and after a successful --lockfile-only the lock is consistent with
          # the manifests by construction (same bun version the build uses).
          # Any failure below hard-aborts with the baseline untouched.
          echo "::group::dependency-consistency gate"
          node scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs
          echo "::endgroup::"

          echo "::group::regenerate bun.lock to match merged manifests"
          export SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1
          LOCKLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/bun-lockfile.log"
          set +e
          bun install --lockfile-only > "$LOCKLOG" 2>&1
          rc=$?
          set -e
          cat "$LOCKLOG"
          if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
            if grep -q "blocked by minimum-release-age" "$LOCKLOG"; then
              echo "::error::Lock regen blocked by bunfig minimumReleaseAge — an upstream dep pin in $NEWTAG is <72h old. This is a timing race, not a breakage: the nightly will succeed once the version ages past the gate (re-run after it ages, or wait for tomorrow's schedule). Baseline left untouched."
            else
              echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            fi
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
          printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
          git add -A
          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present."
          # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
          git push origin "$CAND"

          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND (version $VERSION). Building next."

  build:
    needs: merge
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
    with:
      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}
      publish: true

  advance:
    needs: [merge, build]
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
          git fetch origin "$CAND"
          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
          git push origin FETCH_HEAD:refs/heads/main
          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."

content.content:
1	# Fork feature manifest
2	
3	This file is the source of truth for **what our fork adds on top of upstream
4	superset**. The nightly upstream-merge workflow greps every marker below against
5	the merged tree; **if any marker is missing the merge is rejected** (a feature
6	was dropped or an upstream change clobbered it) and the build hard-aborts into
7	the recovery loop. Markers are deliberately distinctive identifiers that only
8	exist because of our feature code.
9	
10	When you add a new custom feature, add a marker row here in the same commit.
11	When upstream **deprecates/removes** a feature we mirrored, remove its marker row
12	in the merge that drops it (the only legitimate way a marker leaves this list).
13	
14	## Features
15	
16	| Feature | What it is | Marker |
17	|---|---|---|
18	| Native Windows ARM64 packaging | one-click NSIS installer; ARM64 node-pty/libsql/tokenizers | `npm:@lydell/node-pty`, `bun-windows-arm64` |
19	| Window controls | native `titleBarOverlay` is the sole min/max/close set on Windows | `titleBarOverlay` |
20	| Windows behaviour fixes | child-process patch, cmd.exe fallback, force-foreground, watchdog, WebGL recovery, fast startup | `windows-child-process` |
21	| Agent status dots (Claude + Codex) | per-terminal dot + workspace rollup; host-service POST + JSONL watcher | `pane-map-hook`, `StatusIndicator` |
22	| Claude Stop unhooked from notify.sh | upstream's raw Stop passthrough must never race superset-notify.py (it wiped the blue dot + yellow-hold) | `CLAUDE-STOP-UNHOOKED` |
23	| Compaction shows working | Claude context compaction (manual /compact + auto) drives the yellow dot via PreCompact / SessionStart(source=compact) | `(COMPACT-YELLOW)` |
24	| Team/workflow work shows working | background_tasks[] entry types split the post-turn dot: agent-type work (teammate/subagent/workflow) holds yellow; shell-only goes blue | `(TEAM-YELLOW)` |
25	| Codex-companion job holds the dot | detached codex worker is invisible to background_tasks[]; an active (pid-alive) codex job for the session holds yellow incl. through StopFailure AND the JSONL watcher's stream-idle/API-abort + interrupt self-heal (System 1 mirrors the same check via `codexJobActive` in agent-jsonl-watcher.ts, so a codex-only session can't false-green when the Claude API drops and no StopFailure hook fires) | `_codex_job_active` |
26	| Manual compact restores shell-blue | a manual /compact ending while only a background shell runs restores the BackgroundRunning blue (turn-end snapshot marker) instead of false-greening; the JSONL-watcher re-attach after compaction must not wipe that blue | `_shellbg_marker_path`, `(BLUE-SPECTATOR)` |
27	| ws native modules off | host-service `ws` must use pure-JS mask/unmask — a broken packaged bufferutil wedges WS receivers and kills ALL terminal keyboard input | `WS_NO_BUFFER_UTIL` |
28	| Shell-running blue dot | OSC 133 C/D command-running detection | `scanForOsc133Cd` |
29	| Non-git / multi-repo workspaces | open a non-git folder as a plain workspace | `resolveNonGitFolder` |
30	| Multi-repo branch workspaces | "Open from multi-folder" groups N git repos; "+" fans the same branch out as a worktree per repo under one container workspace | `readMultiRepoConfig`, `createMultiRepoWorkspaceFlow` |
31	| Multi-repo member management | Project Settings "Repositories" section adds/removes member repos of a multi-repo project. Add is lazy (included in NEW branch workspaces only — cleanup already tolerates a missing member worktree); remove force-removes that repo's worktrees from every existing branch container (confirm dialog; repo + branches kept) BEFORE the config rewrite so no orphan worktrees/registrations strand. Min 2 members enforced | `addMultiRepoMember`, `MultiRepoMembersSection` |
32	| Workspace branch label | the open workspace page names its branch top-right in the tab bar (click copies) — the only branch surface a non-git multi-repo container has | `WorkspaceBranchLabel` |
33	| Workspace-create failure toast | every workspace/worktree create failure raises a toast with the actual reason (e.g. a GitHub/network timeout) from the one `recordFailure` chokepoint — so a transient failure no longer reads as a silent "stuck creating → reload" | `(CREATE-FAIL-TOAST)` |
34	| Thread snooze / archive | per-thread timed Snooze + sticky Archive in the sidebar | `getWorkspaceSidebarBucket`, `APP_LAUNCH_ID`, `DashboardSidebarStateSection` |
35	| Sidebar hover-freeze | rows never re-sort while the pointer is over the project list (order applies on leave) | `(HOVER-FREEZE)` |
36	| Terminal links | plain click copies a URL/path, Ctrl/Cmd+click opens; `.html` paths open in Chrome (OS default fallback) | `useLinkClickHint`, `openHtmlInBrowser` |
37	| Agent-hook bash-wrap | Gemini/Cursor `.sh` hooks run via Git-for-Windows bash | `agent-wrappers` |
38	| Kanban board | device-local board mirroring branches + Queued column | `v2KanbanCards`, `KANBAN_QUEUE_COLUMN_ID` |
39	| Kanban Completed column | fixed FINAL column: dropping a card stamps an editable completed date and hides the thread from the sidebar ENTIRELY (drag out un-completes/restores); per-column date filter (all / last calendar month / custom range) for work-done reports; completed cards survive branch deletion as frozen records; main cards can't complete | `KANBAN_COMPLETED_COLUMN_ID` |
40	| Kanban append-only backup | daily write-once JSON snapshot of the board; code can never delete/overwrite one | `writeKanbanBackup` |
41	| Kanban sidebar button toggles | sidebar Kanban press: anywhere → full-screen board; part-screen split → full-screen (closing then reopens that workspace full size); full-screen → close back to the remembered previous page (fallback Workspaces list) | `(KANBAN-TOGGLE)` |
42	| Subagent tool events never stomp the red | a PostToolUse whose payload carries `agent_id` (ran inside a subagent) maps to the red-respecting SubagentActive — background agents' tool completions must not clear a pending AskUserQuestion/permission red; only a main-loop completion does. SubagentStart likewise | `(SUBTOOL-RED)` |
43	| Async background-tool completions never stomp the red | the Workflow / Agent / Task tools spawn background agents and complete on the MAIN loop (no agent_id) WHILE the main loop is blocked on an AskUserQuestion/permission red — so their PostToolUse maps to SubagentActive, not Start, instead of wrongly clearing the pending red (the SUBTOOL-RED sequential-main-loop assumption breaks for async-spawning tools) | `(ASYNC-TOOL-RED)` |
44	| Untagged background-agent tool completions never stomp the red | teammate/fork tool completions (ordinary Read/Bash/Edit/…) stream in attributed to the PARENT session WITHOUT an agent_id, so the SUBTOOL-RED/ASYNC-TOOL-RED checks miss them and they would Start-clear a pending AskUserQuestion red. A `<terminalId>.askq/` DIR holds one marker PER OWNER (the raising agent_id, or `_main`), set synchronously at PreToolUse:AskUserQuestion; while ANY marker exists an untagged ordinary PostToolUse maps to the red-respecting SubagentActive (and refreshes `.bgactive`, since the blocked main loop means that tool is genuine teammate activity). Per-owner so concurrent questions (main + a subagent) are independent. The renderer has ONE permission axis per terminal, so a single CENTRAL GUARD wraps the whole decision and enforces "rendered red ⇔ `.askq` dir non-empty" in one place: it counts owners before and after the inner decision, then (DOWNGRADE) turns any red-CLEARING result (Start, or a green/blue turn-end) into the red-respecting SubagentActive while any owner remains (stamping `.mainstopped` on a held turn-end so the last SubagentStop still finalizes green), and (UPGRADE) turns a NON-clearing result (SubagentActive yellow/agent/codex hold, or a None no-op) into Start when this event removed the LAST owner — so a just-answered/cancelled/reaped question's red can't latch behind a yellow hold. One choke point, so NO branch — answer, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, SubagentStop (incl. its cancelled-with-question and still-other-work paths), the compact paths — can drop a co-pending red OR strand a resolved one. (The native-notification manager likewise only chimes "complete" on Stop and "awaiting" on permission, never on SubagentActive.) An owner's marker is cleared by its own answer (PostToolUse:AskUserQuestion), its SubagentStop, or the main turn boundary (Stop/UserPromptSubmit clear only `_main`); dead non-`_main` owners are reaped against `background_tasks` ids on Stop/SubagentStop (MARKER-RECONCILE-style) so a subagent that died without a clean stop can't pin red; the whole dir clears on session end / API abort / fresh SessionStart (its hook is registered UNSCOPED so the non-compact source is reached), and a main interrupt clears only `_main` (detached teammates survive). The JSONL watcher emits its direct interrupt/abort `Stop` as `SubagentActive` instead while a live `.askq` owner remains (it bypasses the Python guard). Synchronous + per-owner = no timing window and no cross-question clearing; a tool-PERMISSION red (no `.askq` marker) still clears on its approved tool's main-loop Start — no approval-clear regression. SubagentStop of a subagent that stopped WITH its own question still open emits Start (central guard keeps any other owner) so a cancelled subagent question's red can't latch; the watcher's held interrupt also stamps `.mainstopped` so a later SubagentStop still finalizes green; and an askq owner is NEVER reaped from run-marker age alone (a subagent legitimately BLOCKED on an open question has no activity, so age-reaping it would drop a LIVE red) — when `background_tasks` is absent a leaked owner just errs YELLOW until a session/abort/fresh-start boundary clears it. Accepted limits (all inherent to the fire-and-forget hook→POST→last-writer-wins pipeline, not specific to this feature): a generic Notification (tool-permission) red is unguarded (its approval is indistinguishable from a teammate tool without a request id); a sub-ms TOCTOU window (a Start in flight before a question opens); and a stale watcher transcript chunk carrying an old API-abort can clear a question raised during the lag (genuine aborts kill the shared-API subagents, so this is only the narrow abort→recover→new-question-within-one-poll window). Fully closing the last two would need pipeline-wide event sequencing | `(UNTAGGED-BG-RED)` |
45	| Working-cadence events unhooked from notify.sh | upstream's raw `notify.sh` passthrough is NOT registered on Claude's UserPromptSubmit / PostToolUse / PostToolUseFailure either (same fix as CLAUDE-STOP-UNHOOKED). The host maps all three to `Start`, and notify.sh bypasses superset-notify.py's central red guard — in a forks/teammates run the forks' PostToolUse completions stream in every 1-5s and each raw `Start` cleared the main loop's pending AskUserQuestion red to yellow. superset-notify.py owns these for Claude (with agent_id discrimination + the guard), so dropping the raw duplicates loses nothing; only binding (SessionStart/SessionEnd) + the native PermissionRequest stay | `(CLAUDE-WORKING-UNHOOKED)` |
46	| Subagent StopFailure stays scoped | a StopFailure carrying an `agent_id` is ONE subagent/fork aborting on a rate-limit/API error, not the whole Claude session dying — it is handled by a SELF-SCOPED inline branch that drops ONLY that fork's run + question markers and never the shared session state (sibling markers, the main `_main` question owner, the agent-bg snapshot), and (like the main-abort branch) never trusts the abort payload's `background_tasks`; the remaining run-marker count + the `.mainstopped` sentinel decide the hold and the central red guard re-holds any still-open question. The full-abort StopFailure branch (clears run_dir + every marker + the `.askq` guard, then greens) would otherwise stomp the main loop's still-pending AskUserQuestion red when a fork hit a limit. Also: superset-notify.py now OWNS `PostToolUseFailure` (aliased to PostToolUse through the red guard) since notify.sh stopped raw-posting it | `(STOPFAIL-SUBAGENT)` |
47	| Layered dot axes | a source's dot status is DERIVED as the highest-precedence active axis (permission > working > review, + the separate blue axes) — events latch/unlatch axes they have evidence about, so a lower assert can never overwrite a higher active state | `applySourceAxes` |
48	| Leaked yellow-hold markers self-heal | a SubagentStop arriving with a mismatched/missing agent_id leaks its run-dir marker and pins the dot yellow with nothing running; at every Stop/SubagentStop the payload's background_tasks[] (ground truth) reaps any marker not listed as still running | `(MARKER-RECONCILE)` |
49	| Subagent markers track per-marker activity | each subagent's run-dir marker is touched on every PostToolUse it fires (not just SubagentStart), so its mtime reflects last activity and the conservative 12h leaked-marker backstop can't false-green a still-working subagent; the COMMON orphan-on-API-error leak is handled fast by (API-ABORT-RELEASE) instead of a flaky short timer | `(MARKER-INACTIVE)` |
50	| Orphaned subagent markers reaped on API abort | a main Claude session's stream abort (`API Error: Stream idle timeout - partial response received`, an isApiErrorMessage line) ends the turn and ORPHANS in-flight subagents WITHOUT firing their SubagentStop, leaking run-dir markers that pin the dot yellow; no hook fires on the abort, so the JSONL watcher (the only thing that reads the transcript) reaps the orphaned per-subagent markers and emits review — deterministic, event-driven, no timer (survivors stay held via the next Stop's background_tasks[]) | `(API-ABORT-RELEASE)` |
51	| Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot; the background-running blue has no self-heal until the next turn end, so a reload used to hide a running background task for hours. Clears on real app restart (no stale dots across launches) | `(DOT-PERSIST)` |
52	| Agent hook fork-diet | every agent lifecycle hook parses + escapes JSON with bash builtins (read, `[[ =~ ]]`, parameter expansion); the old grep/tr + sed pipelines forked ~30 subprocesses per call and crashed the x64-emulated msys2 runtime on Windows ARM64 (the add_item errno-1 fork cascade that wedged every chat's hooks). Wire payload unchanged | `(HOOK-FORK-DIET)` |
53	| Master cards archive-only | a repo's master / non-git master card (`type === "main"`) can never be hard-removed from the sidebar — "Remove from Sidebar" archives it (recoverable under the project's Archived section) instead of hiding it into oblivion. `archiveWorkspace` inserts an archived row for an auto-included main that has none yet | `(MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY)` |
54	| Recycle Bin (soft delete) | the default "Delete" for a worktree/branch workspace (sidebar + kanban, incl. unbound Queued cards) is now a lossless, recoverable move-to-bin (silent — no dialog/toast; worktree + branch + terminals untouched, exactly like Archive). Restore always returns to ACTIVE; nothing is ever auto-purged. Permanent git destroy is relocated to "Delete permanently" inside the bin (the old destroy dialog) + a one-confirm "Empty Recycle Bin". A renderer-only retention setting (default 30 days) is a DISPLAY filter only — the bin shows items deleted within the last N days, older ones collapse behind a per-bin "Show all" toggle with an "N hidden by filter" footer. Per-project sidebar "Recycle Bin (N)" section + per-column kanban sub-section, sorted by `deletedAt` DESC. Mains stay archive-only (never deletable) | `(RECYCLE-BIN)` |
55	| Auto-resume stuck chats | when a Claude chat dies on an API failure the JSONL watcher classifies it (deny-list-first: rate-limit-with-reset-time / transient-rate-limit / server-error / connection-drop / half-stop are resumable; auth/policy/bad-image/model-unavailable are notify-only) after a finality/quiescence gate, and a durable MAIN-side registry auto-sends "resume from exactly where everything was left" — fire-at-parsed-reset-time (+30s, DST-correct, fire-now-if-already-past, ≤8d sane cap) for usage limits, else a 60s→×3 backoff capped at 5 sends / 24h. Every send is gated at fire time (toggle on, still the last meaningful transcript line, host-service `commandRunning`=idle + agent-session binding match) so it can never auto-confirm a menu, land mid-turn, or run at a bare shell. Any trusted interaction with that terminal cancels (persisted tombstone survives reload/restart). Account-wide cohorts are jittered + concurrency-capped. Global toggle (default on). Claude-only in v1 — Codex auto-resume is deferred (the on-disk corpus has no validated standalone usage-limit signal). Dots untouched (separate IPC variant + toasts) | `(AUTO-RESUME)`, `autoResumeManager`, `writeInputIfIdleSession`, `useAutoResumeActivity`, `AutoResumeController` |
56	| Dot-decision log + codex pid-reuse cap | superset-notify.py writes an always-on bounded `~/.superset/logs/dot-decisions.log` line at each terminal turn-end decision (the POSTed eventType + WHY a hold won — codex job / background_tasks / subagent markers / GREEN / BLUE / RED), and `_codex_job_active` age-caps pid-bearing `running` codex job records at 6h so a recycled PID can't pin the dot yellow forever (a cap-skipped stale record is logged, so a premature-green is auditable) | `_decision_log` |
57	| Idle/zombie teammate hold reaped | an agent set Claude Code still reports `running` in background_tasks[] but that has produced NO activity (SubagentStart / subagent-scoped PostToolUse refresh a `.bgactive` timer) for 15 min stops pinning the lead's dot yellow — the harness never flips an idle long-lived teammate (or one that died without a clean SubagentStop) to a finished status, so the hold previously lasted until the team was disbanded / the session ended. The JSONL watcher re-asserts yellow if a reaped set is in fact still writing | `(BG-STALE)` |
58	| Idle teammates green deterministically | Claude Code reports finished teammates in the Stop payload's background_tasks[] as `status:"running"` FOREVER (captured live: 33 teammate entries, all "running", for a session whose 44 teammates had all finished hours earlier) — and the (BG-STALE) reap only runs inside hook events, so a session whose LAST event landed while the set was <15 min idle latched yellow indefinitely (nothing ever re-evaluated; 9 terminals observed stuck). superset-notify.py now keeps a per-terminal teammate LEDGER parsed incrementally from the lead session transcript (payload `transcript_path`; offset+state cached in `<terminalId>.teamstate.json`, keyed by transcript path so /resume self-invalidates): Agent-spawn / SendMessage-wake / non-idle teammate- or agent-messages mark a name active, an `idle_notification` teammate-message marks it idle. At Stop/SubagentStop, when EVERY tracked teammate is provably idle, unfinished teammate-type entries are dropped BEFORE the split/snapshot/hold decision — the lead greens (or blues on a shell remainder) at its actual final turn end instead of holding forever. Positive proof only: no transcript / no teammates seen / any parse error / any teammate not idle → entries kept (old behaviour + (BG-STALE) unchanged behind it); raw fork agentIds ("a"+hex) are excluded from the predicate (forks have real hook lifecycles and would never idle-notify); subagent/workflow/shell entries always pass through untouched; bg_ids marker reconciliation still uses the RAW list. Decision log carries a `[teammate-idle: dropped N ...]` suffix for auditability | `(TEAMMATE-IDLE)` |
59	| ReferenceError-class build gate | the build (and therefore every nightly, whose build job calls the same workflow) fails on cannot-find-name tsc diagnostics ONLY (TS2304/2552/2662/2663/18004) — bare identifiers esbuild bundles fine but that throw ReferenceError at runtime, exactly what a CLEAN-merging upstream hunk can leave in fork code the AI resolver never sees (v1.14.0 layout.tsx incident: upstream renamed the workspace source variable in a non-conflicting hunk; fork's cache-first hold kept referencing it; every v2 workspace view crashed). Per-package direct tsc (turbo graph is debt-blocked); the accepted type debt has zero of these codes so the gate can't false-abort | `(REFERR-GATE)` |
60	| Nightly dependency-consistency + lock regen | the nightly merge can introduce dependency-state drift that only the build discovers (after a windows-arm64 runner spins up): a stale fork root `overrides` entry that pins a package to a version different from the merged workspace pin (electron-builder "production dependency not found …"), or a `bun.lock` left stale by an upstream package.json bump (frozen-install fails). The merge now runs an override-consistency gate (fails loud on any exact override↔pin mismatch) + regenerates `bun.lock` to match the merged manifests with the build's exact bun version (which also fails loud, in the cheap merge job, if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin is age-blocked) — so this class of build failure is caught/fixed before the windows-arm64 build spins up, baseline untouched on failure (recovery loop). No full `--frozen-lockfile` install runs here: the merge runner is linux, so a platform-specific install quirk would be a false abort, and the regenerated lock is consistent with the manifests by construction. The fork carries NO mastra `overrides` and pins `@mastra/core`/`mastracode` to upstream's exact versions in all three workspaces (upstream keeps mastra consistent via identical exact pins; the seed commit's redundant override + a recovery merge that resolved the pins DOWN to 0.18.0 were the recurring breakage, both corrected) | `(LOCK-REGEN)` |
61	
62	## Machine-readable markers (the nightly gate reads this block)
63	
64	Format per line: `<marker-token>\t<path-glob-root>`. The gate greps the token
65	under the path root in the merged tree; absence = fail.
66	
67	```markers
68	npm:@lydell/node-pty	packages
69	bun-windows-arm64	apps/desktop/scripts
70	titleBarOverlay	apps/desktop/src/main
71	windows-child-process	apps/desktop/src/main
72	pane-map-hook	apps/desktop/src/main
73	CLAUDE-STOP-UNHOOKED	apps/desktop/src/main
74	(COMPACT-YELLOW)	apps/desktop/src/main
75	(TEAM-YELLOW)	apps/desktop/src/main
76	_codex_job_active	apps/desktop/src/main
77	_shellbg_marker_path	apps/desktop/src/main
78	(BLUE-SPECTATOR)	apps/desktop/src/renderer
79	WS_NO_BUFFER_UTIL	apps/desktop/src/main
80	WS_NO_BUFFER_UTIL	packages/host-service
81	scanForOsc133Cd	packages
82	StatusIndicator	apps/desktop/src/renderer
83	resolveNonGitFolder	packages/host-service
84	readMultiRepoConfig	packages/host-service
85	createMultiRepoWorkspaceFlow	packages/host-service
86	MultiFolderProjectModal	apps/desktop/src/renderer
87	addMultiRepoMember	packages/host-service
88	MultiRepoMembersSection	apps/desktop/src/renderer

# Fork feature manifest

This file is the source of truth for **what our fork adds on top of upstream
superset**. The nightly upstream-merge workflow greps every marker below against
the merged tree; **if any marker is missing the merge is rejected** (a feature
was dropped or an upstream change clobbered it) and the build hard-aborts into
the recovery loop. Markers are deliberately distinctive identifiers that only
exist because of our feature code.

When you add a new custom feature, add a marker row here in the same commit.
When upstream **deprecates/removes** a feature we mirrored, remove its marker row
in the merge that drops it (the only legitimate way a marker leaves this list).

## Features

| Feature | What it is | Marker |
|---|---|---|
| Native Windows ARM64 packaging | one-click NSIS installer; ARM64 node-pty/libsql/tokenizers | `npm:@lydell/node-pty`, `bun-windows-arm64` |
| Window controls | native `titleBarOverlay` is the sole min/max/close set on Windows | `titleBarOverlay` |
| Windows behaviour fixes | child-process patch, cmd.exe fallback, force-foreground, watchdog, WebGL recovery, fast startup | `windows-child-process` |
| Agent status dots (Claude + Codex) | per-terminal dot + workspace rollup; host-service POST + JSONL watcher | `pane-map-hook`, `StatusIndicator` |
| Claude Stop unhooked from notify.sh | upstream's raw Stop passthrough must never race superset-notify.py (it wiped the blue dot + yellow-hold) | `CLAUDE-STOP-UNHOOKED` |
| Compaction shows working | Claude context compaction (manual /compact + auto) drives the yellow dot via PreCompact / SessionStart(source=compact) | `(COMPACT-YELLOW)` |
| Team/workflow work shows working | background_tasks[] entry types split the post-turn dot: agent-type work (teammate/subagent/workflow) holds yellow; shell-only goes blue | `(TEAM-YELLOW)` |
| Codex-companion job holds the dot | detached codex worker is invisible to background_tasks[]; an active (pid-alive) codex job for the session holds yellow incl. through StopFailure AND the JSONL watcher's stream-idle/API-abort + interrupt self-heal (System 1 mirrors the same check via `codexJobActive` in agent-jsonl-watcher.ts, so a codex-only session can't false-green when the Claude API drops and no StopFailure hook fires) | `_codex_job_active` |
| Manual compact restores shell-blue | a manual /compact ending while only a background shell runs restores the BackgroundRunning blue (turn-end snapshot marker) instead of false-greening; the JSONL-watcher re-attach after compaction must not wipe that blue | `_shellbg_marker_path`, `(BLUE-SPECTATOR)` |
| ws native modules off | host-service `ws` must use pure-JS mask/unmask — a broken packaged bufferutil wedges WS receivers and kills ALL terminal keyboard input | `WS_NO_BUFFER_UTIL` |
| Shell-running blue dot | OSC 133 C/D command-running detection | `scanForOsc133Cd` |
| Non-git / multi-repo workspaces | open a non-git folder as a plain workspace | `resolveNonGitFolder` |
| Multi-repo branch workspaces | "Open from multi-folder" groups N git repos; "+" fans the same branch out as a worktree per repo under one container workspace | `readMultiRepoConfig`, `createMultiRepoWorkspaceFlow` |
| Multi-repo member management | Project Settings "Repositories" section adds/removes member repos of a multi-repo project. Add is lazy (included in NEW branch workspaces only — cleanup already tolerates a missing member worktree); remove force-removes that repo's worktrees from every existing branch container (confirm dialog; repo + branches kept) BEFORE the config rewrite so no orphan worktrees/registrations strand. Min 2 members enforced | `addMultiRepoMember`, `MultiRepoMembersSection` |
| Workspace branch label | the open workspace page names its branch top-right in the tab bar (click copies) — the only branch surface a non-git multi-repo container has | `WorkspaceBranchLabel` |
| Workspace-create failure toast | every workspace/worktree create failure raises a toast with the actual reason (e.g. a GitHub/network timeout) from the one `recordFailure` chokepoint — so a transient failure no longer reads as a silent "stuck creating → reload" | `(CREATE-FAIL-TOAST)` |
| Thread snooze / archive | per-thread timed Snooze + sticky Archive in the sidebar | `getWorkspaceSidebarBucket`, `APP_LAUNCH_ID`, `DashboardSidebarStateSection` |
| Sidebar hover-freeze | rows never re-sort while the pointer is over the project list (order applies on leave) | `(HOVER-FREEZE)` |
| Terminal links | plain click copies a URL/path, Ctrl/Cmd+click opens; `.html` paths open in Chrome (OS default fallback) | `useLinkClickHint`, `openHtmlInBrowser` |
| Agent-hook bash-wrap | Gemini/Cursor `.sh` hooks run via Git-for-Windows bash | `agent-wrappers` |
| Kanban board | device-local board mirroring branches + Queued column | `v2KanbanCards`, `KANBAN_QUEUE_COLUMN_ID` |
| Kanban Completed column | fixed FINAL column: dropping a card stamps an editable completed date and hides the thread from the sidebar ENTIRELY (drag out un-completes/restores); per-column date filter (all / last calendar month / custom range) for work-done reports; completed cards survive branch deletion as frozen records; main cards can't complete | `KANBAN_COMPLETED_COLUMN_ID` |
| Kanban append-only backup | daily write-once JSON snapshot of the board; code can never delete/overwrite one | `writeKanbanBackup` |
| Kanban sidebar button toggles | sidebar Kanban press: anywhere → full-screen board; part-screen split → full-screen (closing then reopens that workspace full size); full-screen → close back to the remembered previous page (fallback Workspaces list) | `(KANBAN-TOGGLE)` |
| Subagent tool events never stomp the red | a PostToolUse whose payload carries `agent_id` (ran inside a subagent) maps to the red-respecting SubagentActive — background agents' tool completions must not clear a pending AskUserQuestion/permission red; only a main-loop completion does. SubagentStart likewise | `(SUBTOOL-RED)` |
| Async background-tool completions never stomp the red | the Workflow / Agent / Task tools spawn background agents and complete on the MAIN loop (no agent_id) WHILE the main loop is blocked on an AskUserQuestion/permission red — so their PostToolUse maps to SubagentActive, not Start, instead of wrongly clearing the pending red (the SUBTOOL-RED sequential-main-loop assumption breaks for async-spawning tools) | `(ASYNC-TOOL-RED)` |
| Untagged background-agent tool completions never stomp the red | teammate/fork tool completions (ordinary Read/Bash/Edit/…) stream in attributed to the PARENT session WITHOUT an agent_id, so the SUBTOOL-RED/ASYNC-TOOL-RED checks miss them and they would Start-clear a pending AskUserQuestion red. A `<terminalId>.askq/` DIR holds one marker PER OWNER (the raising agent_id, or `_main`), set synchronously at PreToolUse:AskUserQuestion; while ANY marker exists an untagged ordinary PostToolUse maps to the red-respecting SubagentActive (and refreshes `.bgactive`, since the blocked main loop means that tool is genuine teammate activity). Per-owner so concurrent questions (main + a subagent) are independent. The renderer has ONE permission axis per terminal, so a single CENTRAL GUARD wraps the whole decision and enforces "rendered red ⇔ `.askq` dir non-empty" in one place: it counts owners before and after the inner decision, then (DOWNGRADE) turns any red-CLEARING result (Start, or a green/blue turn-end) into the red-respecting SubagentActive while any owner remains (stamping `.mainstopped` on a held turn-end so the last SubagentStop still finalizes green), and (UPGRADE) turns a NON-clearing result (SubagentActive yellow/agent/codex hold, or a None no-op) into Start when this event removed the LAST owner — so a just-answered/cancelled/reaped question's red can't latch behind a yellow hold. One choke point, so NO branch — answer, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, SubagentStop (incl. its cancelled-with-question and still-other-work paths), the compact paths — can drop a co-pending red OR strand a resolved one. (The native-notification manager likewise only chimes "complete" on Stop and "awaiting" on permission, never on SubagentActive.) An owner's marker is cleared by its own answer (PostToolUse:AskUserQuestion), its SubagentStop, or the main turn boundary (Stop/UserPromptSubmit clear only `_main`); dead non-`_main` owners are reaped against `background_tasks` ids on Stop/SubagentStop (MARKER-RECONCILE-style) so a subagent that died without a clean stop can't pin red; the whole dir clears on session end / API abort / fresh SessionStart (its hook is registered UNSCOPED so the non-compact source is reached), and a main interrupt clears only `_main` (detached teammates survive). The JSONL watcher emits its direct interrupt/abort `Stop` as `SubagentActive` instead while a live `.askq` owner remains (it bypasses the Python guard). Synchronous + per-owner = no timing window and no cross-question clearing; a tool-PERMISSION red (no `.askq` marker) still clears on its approved tool's main-loop Start — no approval-clear regression. SubagentStop of a subagent that stopped WITH its own question still open emits Start (central guard keeps any other owner) so a cancelled subagent question's red can't latch; the watcher's held interrupt also stamps `.mainstopped` so a later SubagentStop still finalizes green; and an askq owner is NEVER reaped from run-marker age alone (a subagent legitimately BLOCKED on an open question has no activity, so age-reaping it would drop a LIVE red) — when `background_tasks` is absent a leaked owner just errs YELLOW until a session/abort/fresh-start boundary clears it. Accepted limits (all inherent to the fire-and-forget hook→POST→last-writer-wins pipeline, not specific to this feature): a generic Notification (tool-permission) red is unguarded (its approval is indistinguishable from a teammate tool without a request id); a sub-ms TOCTOU window (a Start in flight before a question opens); and a stale watcher transcript chunk carrying an old API-abort can clear a question raised during the lag (genuine aborts kill the shared-API subagents, so this is only the narrow abort→recover→new-question-within-one-poll window). Fully closing the last two would need pipeline-wide event sequencing | `(UNTAGGED-BG-RED)` |
| Working-cadence events unhooked from notify.sh | upstream's raw `notify.sh` passthrough is NOT registered on Claude's UserPromptSubmit / PostToolUse / PostToolUseFailure either (same fix as CLAUDE-STOP-UNHOOKED). The host maps all three to `Start`, and notify.sh bypasses superset-notify.py's central red guard — in a forks/teammates run the forks' PostToolUse completions stream in every 1-5s and each raw `Start` cleared the main loop's pending AskUserQuestion red to yellow. superset-notify.py owns these for Claude (with agent_id discrimination + the guard), so dropping the raw duplicates loses nothing; only binding (SessionStart/SessionEnd) + the native PermissionRequest stay | `(CLAUDE-WORKING-UNHOOKED)` |
| Subagent StopFailure stays scoped | a StopFailure carrying an `agent_id` is ONE subagent/fork aborting on a rate-limit/API error, not the whole Claude session dying — it is handled by a SELF-SCOPED inline branch that drops ONLY that fork's run + question markers and never the shared session state (sibling markers, the main `_main` question owner, the agent-bg snapshot), and (like the main-abort branch) never trusts the abort payload's `background_tasks`; the remaining run-marker count + the `.mainstopped` sentinel decide the hold and the central red guard re-holds any still-open question. The full-abort StopFailure branch (clears run_dir + every marker + the `.askq` guard, then greens) would otherwise stomp the main loop's still-pending AskUserQuestion red when a fork hit a limit. Also: superset-notify.py now OWNS `PostToolUseFailure` (aliased to PostToolUse through the red guard) since notify.sh stopped raw-posting it | `(STOPFAIL-SUBAGENT)` |
| Layered dot axes | a source's dot status is DERIVED as the highest-precedence active axis (permission > working > review, + the separate blue axes) — events latch/unlatch axes they have evidence about, so a lower assert can never overwrite a higher active state | `applySourceAxes` |
| Leaked yellow-hold markers self-heal | a SubagentStop arriving with a mismatched/missing agent_id leaks its run-dir marker and pins the dot yellow with nothing running; at every Stop/SubagentStop the payload's background_tasks[] (ground truth) reaps any marker not listed as still running | `(MARKER-RECONCILE)` |
| Subagent markers track per-marker activity | each subagent's run-dir marker is touched on every PostToolUse it fires (not just SubagentStart), so its mtime reflects last activity and the conservative 12h leaked-marker backstop can't false-green a still-working subagent; the COMMON orphan-on-API-error leak is handled fast by (API-ABORT-RELEASE) instead of a flaky short timer | `(MARKER-INACTIVE)` |
| Orphaned subagent markers reaped on API abort | a main Claude session's stream abort (`API Error: Stream idle timeout - partial response received`, an isApiErrorMessage line) ends the turn and ORPHANS in-flight subagents WITHOUT firing their SubagentStop, leaking run-dir markers that pin the dot yellow; no hook fires on the abort, so the JSONL watcher (the only thing that reads the transcript) reaps the orphaned per-subagent markers and emits review — deterministic, event-driven, no timer (survivors stay held via the next Stop's background_tasks[]) | `(API-ABORT-RELEASE)` |
| Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot; the background-running blue has no self-heal until the next turn end, so a reload used to hide a running background task for hours. Clears on real app restart (no stale dots across launches) | `(DOT-PERSIST)` |
| Agent hook fork-diet | every agent lifecycle hook parses + escapes JSON with bash builtins (read, `[[ =~ ]]`, parameter expansion); the old grep/tr + sed pipelines forked ~30 subprocesses per call and crashed the x64-emulated msys2 runtime on Windows ARM64 (the add_item errno-1 fork cascade that wedged every chat's hooks). Wire payload unchanged | `(HOOK-FORK-DIET)` |
| Master cards archive-only | a repo's master / non-git master card (`type === "main"`) can never be hard-removed from the sidebar — "Remove from Sidebar" archives it (recoverable under the project's Archived section) instead of hiding it into oblivion. `archiveWorkspace` inserts an archived row for an auto-included main that has none yet | `(MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY)` |
| Recycle Bin (soft delete) | the default "Delete" for a worktree/branch workspace (sidebar + kanban, incl. unbound Queued cards) is now a lossless, recoverable move-to-bin (silent — no dialog/toast; worktree + branch + terminals untouched, exactly like Archive). Restore always returns to ACTIVE; nothing is ever auto-purged. Permanent git destroy is relocated to "Delete permanently" inside the bin (the old destroy dialog) + a one-confirm "Empty Recycle Bin". A renderer-only retention setting (default 30 days) is a DISPLAY filter only — the bin shows items deleted within the last N days, older ones collapse behind a per-bin "Show all" toggle with an "N hidden by filter" footer. Per-project sidebar "Recycle Bin (N)" section + per-column kanban sub-section, sorted by `deletedAt` DESC. Mains stay archive-only (never deletable) | `(RECYCLE-BIN)` |
| Auto-resume stuck chats | when a Claude chat dies on an API failure the JSONL watcher classifies it (deny-list-first: rate-limit-with-reset-time / transient-rate-limit / server-error / connection-drop / half-stop are resumable; auth/policy/bad-image/model-unavailable are notify-only) after a finality/quiescence gate, and a durable MAIN-side registry auto-sends "resume from exactly where everything was left" — fire-at-parsed-reset-time (+30s, DST-correct, fire-now-if-already-past, ≤8d sane cap) for usage limits, else a 60s→×3 backoff capped at 5 sends / 24h. Every send is gated at fire time (toggle on, still the last meaningful transcript line, host-service `commandRunning`=idle + agent-session binding match) so it can never auto-confirm a menu, land mid-turn, or run at a bare shell. Any trusted interaction with that terminal cancels (persisted tombstone survives reload/restart). Account-wide cohorts are jittered + concurrency-capped. Global toggle (default on). Claude-only in v1 — Codex auto-resume is deferred (the on-disk corpus has no validated standalone usage-limit signal). Dots untouched (separate IPC variant + toasts) | `(AUTO-RESUME)`, `autoResumeManager`, `writeInputIfIdleSession`, `useAutoResumeActivity`, `AutoResumeController` |
| Dot-decision log + codex pid-reuse cap | superset-notify.py writes an always-on bounded `~/.superset/logs/dot-decisions.log` line at each terminal turn-end decision (the POSTed eventType + WHY a hold won — codex job / background_tasks / subagent markers / GREEN / BLUE / RED), and `_codex_job_active` age-caps pid-bearing `running` codex job records at 6h so a recycled PID can't pin the dot yellow forever (a cap-skipped stale record is logged, so a premature-green is auditable) | `_decision_log` |
| Idle/zombie teammate hold reaped | an agent set Claude Code still reports `running` in background_tasks[] but that has produced NO activity (SubagentStart / subagent-scoped PostToolUse refresh a `.bgactive` timer) for 15 min stops pinning the lead's dot yellow — the harness never flips an idle long-lived teammate (or one that died without a clean SubagentStop) to a finished status, so the hold previously lasted until the team was disbanded / the session ended. The JSONL watcher re-asserts yellow if a reaped set is in fact still writing | `(BG-STALE)` |
| Idle teammates green deterministically | Claude Code reports finished teammates in the Stop payload's background_tasks[] as `status:"running"` FOREVER (captured live: 33 teammate entries, all "running", for a session whose 44 teammates had all finished hours earlier) — and the (BG-STALE) reap only runs inside hook events, so a session whose LAST event landed while the set was <15 min idle latched yellow indefinitely (nothing ever re-evaluated; 9 terminals observed stuck). superset-notify.py now keeps a per-terminal teammate LEDGER parsed incrementally from the lead session transcript (payload `transcript_path`; offset+state cached in `<terminalId>.teamstate.json`, keyed by transcript path so /resume self-invalidates): Agent-spawn / SendMessage-wake / non-idle teammate- or agent-messages mark a name active, an `idle_notification` teammate-message marks it idle. At Stop/SubagentStop, when EVERY tracked teammate is provably idle, unfinished teammate-type entries are dropped BEFORE the split/snapshot/hold decision — the lead greens (or blues on a shell remainder) at its actual final turn end instead of holding forever. Positive proof only: no transcript / no teammates seen / any parse error / any teammate not idle → entries kept (old behaviour + (BG-STALE) unchanged behind it); raw fork agentIds ("a"+hex) are excluded from the predicate (forks have real hook lifecycles and would never idle-notify); subagent/workflow/shell entries always pass through untouched; bg_ids marker reconciliation still uses the RAW list. Decision log carries a `[teammate-idle: dropped N ...]` suffix for auditability | `(TEAMMATE-IDLE)` |
| ReferenceError-class build gate | the build (and therefore every nightly, whose build job calls the same workflow) fails on cannot-find-name tsc diagnostics ONLY (TS2304/2552/2662/2663/18004) — bare identifiers esbuild bundles fine but that throw ReferenceError at runtime, exactly what a CLEAN-merging upstream hunk can leave in fork code the AI resolver never sees (v1.14.0 layout.tsx incident: upstream renamed the workspace source variable in a non-conflicting hunk; fork's cache-first hold kept referencing it; every v2 workspace view crashed). Per-package direct tsc (turbo graph is debt-blocked); the accepted type debt has zero of these codes so the gate can't false-abort | `(REFERR-GATE)` |
| Nightly dependency-consistency + lock regen | the nightly merge can introduce dependency-state drift that only the build discovers (after a windows-arm64 runner spins up): a stale fork root `overrides` entry that pins a package to a version different from the merged workspace pin (electron-builder "production dependency not found …"), or a `bun.lock` left stale by an upstream package.json bump (frozen-install fails). The merge now runs an override-consistency gate (fails loud on any exact override↔pin mismatch) + regenerates `bun.lock` to match the merged manifests with the build's exact bun version (which also fails loud, in the cheap merge job, if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin is age-blocked) — so this class of build failure is caught/fixed before the windows-arm64 build spins up, baseline untouched on failure (recovery loop). No full `--frozen-lockfile` install runs here: the merge runner is linux, so a platform-specific install quirk would be a false abort, and the regenerated lock is consistent with the manifests by construction. The fork carries NO mastra `overrides` and pins `@mastra/core`/`mastracode` to upstream's exact versions in all three workspaces (upstream keeps mastra consistent via identical exact pins; the seed commit's redundant override + a recovery merge that resolved the pins DOWN to 0.18.0 were the recurring breakage, both corrected) | `(LOCK-REGEN)` |

lines: +// (PATH-CI-DEDUPE) Windows and macOS filesystems are case-insensitive: the

+// case-variant path silently missed the existing `projects.repoPath` row and
+// the import flow minted a DUPLICATE project (the cloud slug conflict then
+// resolves by suffixing `-2` instead of reusing). Compare case-insensitively
+// on those platforms; keep exact matching on Linux where two paths differing
+// only by case can be genuinely distinct folders.

# The ongoing half of the architecture. Nightly: if upstream superset published a
# newer desktop-v* tag than .fork/upstream-baseline.txt, merge that tag into the
# fork. git's 3-way merge handles the non-conflicting majority deterministically;
# Opus (--effort high) resolves ONLY the conflicted files, preserving every
# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the deterministic ARM64
# build. On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
# fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
# build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline untouched -> recovery loop.
