          # --- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) AI review of the WHOLE merged delta -------
          # The marker gate only proves feature TOKENS still exist, and the
          # build's (REFERR-GATE) only catches cannot-find-name diagnostics. A
          # cleanly-merging upstream hunk can still SEMANTICALLY break a fork
          # feature with zero conflicts — v1.14.2 rebuilt the sidebar row
          # projection in a non-conflicting hunk and silently dropped the fork's
          # snooze/archive/completed/deleted fields: merged clean, compiled
          # clean, broke Snooze + Recycle Bin + archived masters. EVERY merge —
          # clean or conflicted — is therefore reviewed by Opus against
          # FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/publish. Fail CLOSED: no
          # parsable verdict (rate limit, malformed output) aborts rather than
          # ships an unreviewed merge; a BREAKAGE verdict hard-aborts with the
          # findings in the log (baseline untouched -> recovery loop).
          echo "::group::semantic feature review (Opus)"
          npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
          REVIEW_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt"
          rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"
          # The reviewer must not modify the tree; snapshot the work-tree state
          # and refuse to continue if the review run changed ANYTHING.
          STATUS_BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
          REVIEW_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are reviewing an ALREADY-RESOLVED, staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} into our Windows-ARM64 fork. Your ONLY job is to decide whether the merged result SEMANTICALLY breaks any fork feature. Steps: (1) Read FEATURES.md — the fork feature manifest (feature descriptions + marker tokens). (2) Run 'git --no-pager diff --cached --name-only' to list every file the merge changed. (3) For each changed file that participates in a fork feature — it contains a FEATURES.md marker token, or it is in the blast radius of one (e.g. a projection/select/mapping that a feature's fields flow through, a component a feature's props pass through, a hook a feature's guard lives in) — read the staged diff ('git --no-pager diff --cached -- <file>') AND the surrounding file content, and verify the fork behaviour still works end-to-end: fork-added fields still projected/carried, fork branches and guards still reachable, fork props still passed, fork call sites still invoked. An upstream refactor that preserves behaviour is fine; flag only real breakage. (4) Write your verdict to the file at ${REVIEW_FILE} — the FIRST line must be exactly 'VERDICT: OK' or 'VERDICT: BREAKAGE'; for BREAKAGE add one line per finding: '<file> — <feature/marker> — <what broke>'. Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. Output nothing else."
          attempt=1
          while [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
            echo "::group::claude review attempt $attempt"
            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REVIEW_PROMPT" || true
            echo "::endgroup::"
            if [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] && head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then break; fi
            attempt=$((attempt+1))
          done
          STATUS_AFTER=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
          if [ "$STATUS_BEFORE" != "$STATUS_AFTER" ]; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) the review run modified the work tree — refusing to trust or commit it. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          if ! [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] || ! head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) no parsable review verdict for $NEWTAG — refusing to ship an UNREVIEWED merge (fail closed). Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "--- review verdict ---"
          cat "$REVIEW_FILE"
          if head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -q 'BREAKAGE'; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus found fork-feature breakage in the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Findings above. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------

input.new_string: | Master cards archive-only | a repo's master / non-git master card (`type === "main"`) can never be hard-removed from the sidebar — "Remove from Sidebar" archives it (recoverable under the project's Archived section) instead of hiding it into oblivion. `archiveWorkspace` inserts an archived row for an auto-included main that has none yet. lines: | Orphaned subagent markers reaped on API abort | a main Claude session's stream abort (`API Error: Stream idle timeout - partial response received`, an isApiErrorMessage line) ends the turn and ORPHANS in-flight subagents WITHOUT firing their SubagentStop, leaking run-dir markers that pin the dot yellow; no hook fires on the abort, so the JSONL watcher (the only thing that reads the transcript) reaps the orphaned per-subagent markers and emits review — deterministic, event-driven, no timer (survivors stay held via the next Stop's background_tasks[]) | `(API-ABORT-RELEASE)` |

| Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot; the background-running blue has no self-heal until the next turn end, so a reload used to hide a running background task for hours. Clears on real app restart (no stale dots across launches) | `(DOT-PERSIST)` |

| Agent hook fork-diet | every agent lifecycle hook parses + escapes JSON with bash builtins (read, `[[ =~ ]]`, parameter expansion); the old grep/tr + sed pipelines forked ~30 subprocesses per call and crashed the x64-emulated msys2 runtime on Windows ARM64 (the add_item errno-1 fork cascade that wedged every chat's hooks). Wire payload unchanged | `(HOOK-FORK-DIET)` |

-| Master cards archive-only | a repo's master / non-git master card (`type === "main"`) can never be hard-removed from the sidebar — "Remove from Sidebar" archives it (recoverable under the project's Archived section) instead of hiding it into oblivion. `archiveWorkspace` inserts an archived row for an auto-included main that has none yet | `(MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY)` |

+| Master cards archive-only | a repo's master / non-git master card (`type === "main"`) can never be hard-removed from the sidebar — "Remove from Sidebar" archives it (recoverable under the project's Archived section) instead of hiding it into oblivion. `archiveWorkspace` inserts an archived row for an auto-included main that has none yet. The missing-worktree error page offers Archive (not Delete) for mains; the delete/complete guards resolve the workspace type host-record-first and FAIL LOUD (refuse) when the type is unresolvable instead of failing open on a cold cache; Empty Recycle Bin defensively skips mains | `(MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY)` |

| Recycle Bin (soft delete) | the default "Delete" for a worktree/branch workspace (sidebar + kanban, incl. unbound Queued cards) is now a lossless, recoverable move-to-bin (silent — no dialog/toast; worktree + branch + terminals untouched, exactly like Archive). Restore always returns to ACTIVE; nothing is ever auto-purged. Permanent git destroy is relocated to "Delete permanently" inside the bin (the old destroy dialog) + a one-confirm "Empty Recycle Bin". A renderer-only retention setting (default 30 days) is a DISPLAY filter only — the bin shows items deleted within the last N days, older ones collapse behind a per-bin "Show all" toggle with an "N hidden by filter" footer. Per-project sidebar "Recycle Bin (N)" section + per-column kanban sub-section, sorted by `deletedAt` DESC. Mains stay archive-only (never deletable) | `(RECYCLE-BIN)` |

| Auto-resume stuck chats | when a Claude chat dies on an API failure the JSONL watcher classifies it (deny-list-first: rate-limit-with-reset-time / transient-rate-limit / server-error / connection-drop / half-stop are resumable; auth/policy/bad-image/model-unavailable are notify-only) after a finality/quiescence gate, and a durable MAIN-side registry auto-sends "resume from exactly where everything was left" — fire-at-parsed-reset-time (+30s, DST-correct, fire-now-if-already-past, ≤8d sane cap) for usage limits, else a 60s→×3 backoff capped at 5 sends / 24h. Every send is gated at fire time (toggle on, still the last meaningful transcript line, host-service `commandRunning`=idle + agent-session binding match) so it can never auto-confirm a menu, land mid-turn, or run at a bare shell. Any trusted interaction with that terminal cancels (persisted tombstone survives reload/restart). Account-wide cohorts are jittered + concurrency-capped. Global toggle (default on). Claude-only in v1 — Codex auto-resume is deferred (the on-disk corpus has no validated standalone usage-limit signal). Dots untouched (separate IPC variant + toasts) | `(AUTO-RESUME)`, `autoResumeManager`, `writeInputIfIdleSession`, `useAutoResumeActivity`, `AutoResumeController` |

| Dot-decision log + codex pid-reuse cap | superset-notify.py writes an always-on bounded `~/.superset/logs/dot-decisions.log` line at each terminal turn-end decision (the POSTed eventType + WHY a hold won — codex job / background_tasks / subagent markers / GREEN / BLUE / RED), and `_codex_job_active` age-caps pid-bearing `running` codex job records at 6h so a recycled PID can't pin the dot yellow forever (a cap-skipped stale record is logged, so a premature-green is auditable) | `_decision_log` |

input.old_string: | Thread snooze / archive | per-thread timed Snooze + sticky Archive in the sidebar | `getWorkspaceSidebarBucket`, `APP_LAUNCH_ID`, `DashboardSidebarStateSection` |
input.new_string:
| Thread snooze / archive | per-thread timed Snooze + sticky Archive in the sidebar | `getWorkspaceSidebarBucket`, `APP_LAUNCH_ID`, `DashboardSidebarStateSection` |
| Sidebar row projection carries the fork state fields | the host-owned sidebar row projection (`rawSidebarWorkspaces` in `useDashboardSidebarData`) must carry `snoozeUntil`/`snoozeLaunchId`/`archivedAt`/`completedAt`/`deletedAt` from `v2WorkspaceLocalState` — the v1.14.2 upstream merge rebuilt that mapping and silently dropped them, which broke snooze (rows never left the active lane), emptied the Recycle Bin, vanished archived masters, and resurfaced completed threads under Archived | `(SIDEBAR-STATE-PROJECTION)` |
| Master rows snoozeable | `snoozeWorkspace` inserts a pre-snoozed local-state row for an auto-included main that has none yet (mirrors `archiveWorkspace`) — Snooze on a master row used to silently no-op | `(SNOOZE-MAIN)` |
| Sticky project removal | "Remove project from sidebar" tombstones EVERY workspace of the project incl. mains, and passive workspace route mounts (session restore, kanban split) use `placeWorkspaceFromPassiveMount`, which never re-inserts the project's sidebar row and never resurrects an existing hidden row — a removed project can only come back via an EXPLICIT open (Workspaces page, project setup/import) | `(REMOVE-STICKY)` |
| Case-insensitive repo-path dedupe | host-service `project.findByPath` / import-collision checks compare `projects.repoPath` case-insensitively on win32/darwin (case-insensitive filesystems) — a case-only folder rename used to miss the existing row and mint a duplicate project (cloud slug conflict suffixed `-2` instead of reusing) | `(PATH-CI-DEDUPE)` |
| Merge semantic review gate | every nightly upstream merge — clean or conflicted — is reviewed by Opus against this manifest BEFORE it may commit/build/publish: a cleanly-merging upstream hunk that semantically breaks a fork feature (the v1.14.2 sidebar-projection incident) is caught here, not in production. Fail closed: no parsable verdict, a BREAKAGE verdict, or a review run that modified the tree all hard-abort with the baseline untouched | `(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE)` |

| Sidebar hover-freeze | rows never re-sort while the pointer is over the project list (order applies on leave) | `(HOVER-FREEZE)` |

3	<!-- divider is about maintaining this fork; everything BELOW is upstream  -->
4	<!-- superset's own developer guide, unchanged, for working on the app.    -->
5	<!-- ===================================================================== -->
6	
7	# superset-windows-arm64 — Windows ARM64 fork maintenance
8	
9	This repo is a **vendored fork** of [superset-sh/superset]: the full upstream
10	source with our Windows ARM64 + feature changes committed on top, plus CI that
11	builds a native **Windows ARM64** one-click installer. The fork is the source of
12	truth; we track upstream by **merging its deltas**, not by re-applying changes.
13	
14	## Fork architecture
15	
16	- **Baseline (frozen).** The whole fork is committed source. Building it is
17	  deterministic and **AI-free** — `.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml` does
18	  install → compile → materialize the win-arm64 native closure → package the NSIS
19	  installer → verify the packaged natives are ARM64. No upstream clone, no patch
20	  apply, no arch-fixup. `.fork/upstream-baseline.txt` records the upstream tag the
21	  baseline sits on.
22	- **Nightly (minimal AI).** `.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml` runs nightly: if
23	  upstream published a newer `desktop-v*` tag, it `git merge`s it in. Git resolves
24	  the non-conflicting majority deterministically; an **Opus** model (`--effort
25	  high`) resolves **only the conflicted files**, preserving every feature. AI cost
26	  scales with the **upstream delta per release**, NOT with how large the fork grows.
27	- **Gates.** After the merge: every marker in `FEATURES.md` must still be present
28	  (else a feature was dropped → reject), then the deterministic build must go green.
29	  On a clean merge + green build the nightly **auto-publishes the single
30	  `desktop-v<version>` Release** (rebuilt in place — see One-version below) and
31	  advances the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker, build
32	  failure) **hard-aborts** and leaves the baseline untouched → recovery.
33	- **Recovery.** When a nightly merge can't be done cleanly, fix it locally with the
34	  maintainer, rebuild, re-freeze the baseline; the nightly then resumes merging only
35	  *new* upstream changes from that point.
36	
37	## Fork non-negotiables
38	
39	- **Whole feature set or fail loud.** Every `FEATURES.md` marker survives a merge or
40	  the build aborts. Never ship a partial fork.
41	- **v2-only, forever.** The v2 cloud/host-service stack is pinned on; never v1.
42	- **No build-time type/test gate — with ONE narrow exception.** The build runs
43	  electron-vite/esbuild; type/format errors don't fail it (the tree carries
44	  accepted type debt). The exception is `(REFERR-GATE)`
45	  (`scripts/check-dangerous-diagnostics.mjs`, run by the build workflow, and
46	  therefore by every nightly): cannot-find-name tsc diagnostics
47	  (TS2304/2552-class) DO fail the build — bare identifiers that throw
48	  ReferenceError at runtime, the exact damage a cleanly-merging upstream hunk
49	  leaves in fork code the AI resolver never sees (v1.14.0 layout.tsx
50	  incident). Validate + e2e locally before relying on a release.
51	- **AI only in the nightly merge.** The build is AI-free. The nightly conflict
52	  resolver needs `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`; if rate-limited it aborts rather than ship
53	  a half-merged fork.
54	- **One version, ever.** Exactly one GitHub Release per upstream version, tagged
55	  `desktop-v<version>`, **rebuilt in place** (delete + recreate so the tag always
56	  points at the latest build). NO `-beta`, NO prerelease, NO separate release/beta
57	  split — there is just *the* `desktop-v<version>` build.
58	
59	## Fork features (high-level + UX, for a full rebuild)
60	
61	See `FEATURES.md` for the marker manifest. In brief:
62	
63	- **Native Windows ARM64 packaging** — one-click NSIS installer; ARM64 node-pty,
64	  libsql, tokenizers; renderer CORS for `superset-app://`.
65	- **Window controls** — native `titleBarOverlay` is the sole min/max/close set on
66	  Windows, theme-matched; upstream's duplicate controls hidden on Windows.
67	- **Windows behaviour fixes** — skip quit-confirmation; cmd.exe fallback;
68	  force-foreground; hidden-window watchdog; WebGL first-paint recovery; Wispr Flow
69	  accessibility + paste fix (UIA reachable WITHOUT xterm screenReaderMode); fast
70	  non-blocking startup (no main-thread fs).
71	- **Agent status dots (Claude + Codex)** — a coloured dot per terminal + a workspace
72	  rollup: red = needs input, yellow = working (held while subagents run; also while
73	  Claude compacts context — PreCompact/SessionStart(source=compact) bracket it — and
74	  while agent-type background_tasks (teammates/forks/workflows) outlive the turn,
75	  and while a pid-alive codex-companion job for the session runs — held even
76	  through a Claude StopFailure, since codex is on its own API), green = ready
77	  for review, blue = a foreground shell command or a shell-only background
78	  remainder / cloud session (a manual /compact restores this blue from a
79	  turn-end snapshot marker instead of false-greening).
80	  Precedence red > yellow > blue > green. Host-service lifecycle POSTs + a JSONL

<!-- FORK MAINTENANCE (khairm Windows ARM64 fork). Everything ABOVE the -->
<!-- divider is about maintaining this fork; everything BELOW is upstream  -->
<!-- superset's own developer guide, unchanged, for working on the app.    -->
<!-- ===================================================================== -->

# superset-windows-arm64 — Windows ARM64 fork maintenance

This repo is a **vendored fork** of [superset-sh/superset]: the full upstream
source with our Windows ARM64 + feature changes committed on top, plus CI that
builds a native **Windows ARM64** one-click installer. The fork is the source of
truth; we track upstream by **merging its deltas**, not by re-applying changes.

## Fork architecture

- **Baseline (frozen).** The whole fork is committed source. Building it is
  deterministic and **AI-free** — `.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml` does
  install → compile → materialize the win-arm64 native closure → package the NSIS
  installer → verify the packaged natives are ARM64. No upstream clone, no patch
  apply, no arch-fixup. `.fork/upstream-baseline.txt` records the upstream tag the
  baseline sits on.
- **Nightly (minimal AI).** `.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml` runs nightly: if
  upstream published a newer `desktop-v*` tag, it `git merge`s it in. Git resolves
  the non-conflicting majority deterministically; an **Opus** model (`--effort
  high`) resolves **only the conflicted files**, preserving every feature. AI cost
  scales with the **upstream delta per release**, NOT with how large the fork grows.
- **Gates.** After the merge: every marker in `FEATURES.md` must still be present
  (else a feature was dropped → reject), then the deterministic build must go green.
  On a clean merge + green build the nightly **auto-publishes the single
  `desktop-v<version>` Release** (rebuilt in place — see One-version below) and
  advances the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker, build
  failure) **hard-aborts** and leaves the baseline untouched → recovery.
- **Recovery.** When a nightly merge can't be done cleanly, fix it locally with the
  maintainer, rebuild, re-freeze the baseline; the nightly then resumes merging only
  *new* upstream changes from that point.

## Fork non-negotiables

- **Whole feature set or fail loud.** Every `FEATURES.md` marker survives a merge or
  the build aborts. Never ship a partial fork.
- **v2-only, forever.** The v2 cloud/host-service stack is pinned on; never v1.
- **No build-time type/test gate — with ONE narrow exception.** The build runs
  electron-vite/esbuild; type/format errors don't fail it (the tree carries
  accepted type debt). The exception is `(REFERR-GATE)`
  (`scripts/check-dangerous-diagnostics.mjs`, run by the build workflow, and
  therefore by every nightly): cannot-find-name tsc diagnostics
  (TS2304/2552-class) DO fail the build — bare identifiers that throw
  ReferenceError at runtime, the exact damage a cleanly-merging upstream hunk
  leaves in fork code the AI resolver never sees (v1.14.0 layout.tsx
  incident). Validate + e2e locally before relying on a release.
- **AI only in the nightly merge.** The build is AI-free. The nightly conflict
  resolver needs `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`; if rate-limited it aborts rather than ship
  a half-merged fork.
- **One version, ever.** Exactly one GitHub Release per upstream version, tagged
  `desktop-v<version>`, **rebuilt in place** (delete + recreate so the tag always
  points at the latest build). NO `-beta`, NO prerelease, NO separate release/beta
  split — there is just *the* `desktop-v<version>` build.

## Fork features (high-level + UX, for a full rebuild)

See `FEATURES.md` for the marker manifest. In brief:

- **Native Windows ARM64 packaging** — one-click NSIS installer; ARM64 node-pty,
  libsql, tokenizers; renderer CORS for `superset-app://`.
- **Window controls** — native `titleBarOverlay` is the sole min/max/close set on
  Windows, theme-matched; upstream's duplicate controls hidden on Windows.
- **Windows behaviour fixes** — skip quit-confirmation; cmd.exe fallback;
  force-foreground; hidden-window watchdog; WebGL first-paint recovery; Wispr Flow
  accessibility + paste fix (UIA reachable WITHOUT xterm screenReaderMode); fast
  non-blocking startup (no main-thread fs).
- **Agent status dots (Claude + Codex)** — a coloured dot per terminal + a workspace
  rollup: red = needs input, yellow = working (held while subagents run; also while
  Claude compacts context — PreCompact/SessionStart(source=compact) bracket it — and
  while agent-type background_tasks (teammates/forks/workflows) outlive the turn,
  and while a pid-alive codex-companion job for the session runs — held even
  through a Claude StopFailure, since codex is on its own API), green = ready
  for review, blue = a foreground shell command or a shell-only background
  remainder / cloud session (a manual /compact restores this blue from a
  turn-end snapshot marker instead of false-greening).
  Precedence red > yellow > blue > green. Host-service lifecycle POSTs + a JSONL

1	import type { AppRouter } from "@superset/host-service";
2	import type { LayoutNode, Tab, WorkspaceState } from "@superset/panes";
3	import type { inferRouterInputs } from "@trpc/server";
4	import { z } from "zod";
5	
6	const persistedDateSchema = z
7		.union([z.string(), z.date()])
8		.transform((value) => (typeof value === "string" ? new Date(value) : value));
9	
10	export const dashboardSidebarProjectSchema = z.object({
11		projectId: z.string().uuid(),
12		createdAt: persistedDateSchema,
13		isCollapsed: z.boolean().default(false),
14		// (ACTIVE-FIRST) Manual right-click pin. Pinned projects form the top sort
15		// tier (pinned > active > idle); within the tier they keep their manual drag
16		// order. Local-only like every other sidebar preference; legacy rows lack the
17		// key and read back undefined, so the data hook heals it to false.
18		isPinned: z.boolean().default(false),
19		tabOrder: z.number().int().default(0),
20		defaultOpenInApp: z.string().nullable().default(null),
21		// Per-project reveal state for the Snoozed / Archived sections. Each
22		// section is hidden until the user explicitly reveals it (right-click the
23		// project), and once revealed can be collapsed independently. Remembered
24		// across restarts (local-only, like every other sidebar preference).
25		showSnoozed: z.boolean().default(false),
26		showArchived: z.boolean().default(false),
27		snoozedCollapsed: z.boolean().default(false),
28		archivedCollapsed: z.boolean().default(false),
29		// (RECYCLE-BIN) Per-project reveal + collapse for the Recycle Bin section
30		// (soft-deleted threads), mirroring the Snoozed / Archived flags above.
31		showDeleted: z.boolean().default(false),
32		deletedCollapsed: z.boolean().default(false),
33	});
34	
35	const paneWorkspaceStateSchema = z.custom<WorkspaceState<unknown>>();
36	
37	// Structural validators for the persisted pane layout. `paneWorkspaceStateSchema`
38	// above is a permissive passthrough for writes (the store always produces a
39	// valid shape); these run on READ via `sanitizePaneLayout` so a malformed or
40	// legacy-shaped layout (e.g. the pre-binary-tree `{ panes, focusedPaneId }`
41	// shape) is healed instead of feeding an undefined node to the renderer.
42	const layoutNodeSchema: z.ZodType<LayoutNode> = z.lazy(() =>
43		z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
44			z.object({ type: z.literal("pane"), paneId: z.string() }),
45			z.object({
46				type: z.literal("split"),
47				direction: z.enum(["horizontal", "vertical"]),
48				first: layoutNodeSchema,
49				second: layoutNodeSchema,
50				splitPercentage: z.number().optional(),
51			}),
52		]),
53	);
54	
55	const paneNodeSchema = z.object({
56		id: z.string(),
57		kind: z.string(),
58		titleOverride: z.string().optional(),
59		pinned: z.boolean().optional(),
60		data: z.unknown(),
61	});
62	
63	const tabNodeSchema = z.object({
64		id: z.string(),
65		titleOverride: z.string().optional(),
66		createdAt: z.number(),
67		activePaneId: z.string().nullable(),
68		layout: layoutNodeSchema,
69		panes: z.record(z.string(), paneNodeSchema),
70	});
71	
72	const EMPTY_PANE_LAYOUT: WorkspaceState<unknown> = {
73		version: 1,
74		tabs: [],
75		activeTabId: null,
76	};
77	
78	/**
79	 * Read-time heal for a persisted pane layout. An unparseable top-level shape
80	 * (missing `version`/`tabs`, or the legacy `{ panes, focusedPaneId }` layout)
81	 * resets to empty; individually-corrupt tabs (e.g. a split node missing a
82	 * child) are dropped while valid tabs are kept, and `activeTabId` is repaired
83	 * to point at a surviving tab. Prevents the renderer from rendering an
84	 * undefined layout node.
85	 */
86	export function sanitizePaneLayout(raw: unknown): WorkspaceState<unknown> {
87		if (!raw || typeof raw !== "object") return EMPTY_PANE_LAYOUT;
88		const value = raw as Record<string, unknown>;
89		if (value.version !== 1 || !Array.isArray(value.tabs)) {
90			return EMPTY_PANE_LAYOUT;
91		}
92		const tabs = value.tabs.flatMap((tab): Tab<unknown>[] => {
93			const parsed = tabNodeSchema.safeParse(tab);
94			return parsed.success ? [parsed.data as Tab<unknown>] : [];
95		});
96		const activeTabId =
97			typeof value.activeTabId === "string" &&
98			tabs.some((tab) => tab.id === value.activeTabId)
99				? value.activeTabId
100				: (tabs[0]?.id ?? null);
101		return { version: 1, tabs, activeTabId };
102	}
103	
104	const changesFilterSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("kind", [
105		z.object({ kind: z.literal("all") }),
106		z.object({ kind: z.literal("uncommitted") }),
107		z.object({ kind: z.literal("commit"), hash: z.string() }),
108		z.object({
109			kind: z.literal("range"),
110			fromHash: z.string(),
111			toHash: z.string(),
112		}),
113	]);
114	
115	export type ChangesFilter = z.infer<typeof changesFilterSchema>;
116	
117	export type ChangesViewMode = "folders" | "tree";
118	
119	const workspaceRunStateSchema = z.enum([
120		"running",
121		"stopped-by-user",
122		"stopped-by-exit",
123	]);
124	
125	export const workspaceRunTerminalStateSchema = z.object({
126		terminalId: z.string(),
127		workspaceId: z.string().uuid(),
128		state: workspaceRunStateSchema,
129		command: z.string(),
130		definitionSource: z.enum(["project-config", "terminal-preset"]),
131		definitionId: z.string().optional(),
132		startedAt: z.number(),
133		stoppedAt: z.number().optional(),
134		exitCode: z.number().optional(),
135		signal: z.number().optional(),
136		stopRequestedAt: z.number().optional(),
137	});
138	
139	export const workspaceLocalStateSchema = z.object({
140		workspaceId: z.string().uuid(),
141		createdAt: persistedDateSchema,
142		sidebarState: z.object({
143			projectId: z.string().uuid(),
144			tabOrder: z.number().int().default(0),
145			sectionId: z.string().uuid().nullable().default(null),
146			changesFilter: changesFilterSchema.default({ kind: "all" }),
147			changesViewMode: z.enum(["folders", "tree"]).default("folders"),
148			// "card" (KANBAN) is the right-panel Task/Card tab added alongside
149			// Files/Changes/Review. Additive enum widening — older rows still read
150			// "changes". Default stays "changes" (the Card tab never auto-selects).
151			activeTab: z
152				.enum(["changes", "files", "review", "card"])
153				.default("changes"),
154			// `isHidden` doubles as the ARCHIVED flag — an archived thread is hidden
155			// from the active lane and surfaced under the project's Archived section.
156			// `archivedAt` orders that section (most-recently-archived first); legacy
157			// hidden rows have no timestamp and sort last. `snooze*` is the timed-hide
158			// state (mutually exclusive with archived): a thread is snoozed while
159			// `snoozeUntil` is in the future, or `snoozeLaunchId` matches the current
160			// app launch ("until next launch"). All local-only, visual-only.
161			isHidden: z.boolean().default(false),
162			archivedAt: z.number().nullable().default(null),
163			snoozeUntil: z.number().nullable().default(null),
164			snoozeLaunchId: z.string().nullable().default(null),
165			// (KANBAN COMPLETED) Set when the branch's kanban card sits in the fixed
166			// Completed column. Presence hides the thread row from the sidebar
167			// ENTIRELY (no section — the board is its only surface). completeWorkspace
168			// also sets isHidden so raw-visibility consumers (notifications, ports)
169			// treat it like an archived row; the bucket classifier checks completedAt
170			// FIRST so it never surfaces under Archived.
171			completedAt: z.number().nullable().default(null),
172			// (RECYCLE-BIN) Soft-delete timestamp. Set by deleteWorkspace (the default
173			// "Delete" now moves a thread to the project's Recycle Bin instead of the
174			// hard git-destroy). Visual-only and fully lossless — the worktree, branch
175			// and running terminals are untouched, exactly like Archive. Presence makes
176			// the bucket classifier return "deleted" FIRST (before completed/archived),
177			// so a deleted thread shows ONLY in the bin. restoreWorkspace clears it back
178			// to active. The permanent git-destroy lives behind "Delete permanently"
179			// inside the bin.
180			deletedAt: z.number().nullable().default(null),
181		}),
182		paneLayout: paneWorkspaceStateSchema,
183		viewedFiles: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
184		recentlyViewedFiles: z
185			.array(
186				z.object({
187					relativePath: z.string(),
188					absolutePath: z.string(),
189					lastAccessedAt: z.number(),
190				}),
191			)
192			.default([]),
193		workspaceRunTerminals: z
194			.record(z.string(), workspaceRunTerminalStateSchema)
195			.default({}),
196	});
197	
198	// Defaults for fields heal can synthesize. Identity fields (workspaceId,
199	// createdAt, paneLayout, sidebarState.projectId) intentionally absent — they
200	// must come from the stored row.
201	const SIDEBAR_STATE_DEFAULTS = {
202		tabOrder: 0,
203		sectionId: null,
204		changesFilter: { kind: "all" },
205		changesViewMode: "folders",
206		activeTab: "changes",
207		isHidden: false,
208		archivedAt: null,
209		snoozeUntil: null,
210		snoozeLaunchId: null,
211		completedAt: null,
212		deletedAt: null,
213	} as const;
214	
215	const WORKSPACE_LOCAL_STATE_OPTIONAL_DEFAULTS = {
216		viewedFiles: [] as string[],
217		recentlyViewedFiles: [] as Array<{
218			relativePath: string;
219			absolutePath: string;
220			lastAccessedAt: number;
221		}>,
222		workspaceRunTerminals: {} as Record<
223			string,
224			z.infer<typeof workspaceRunTerminalStateSchema>
225		>,
226	};
227	
228	export const dashboardSidebarSectionSchema = z.object({
229		sectionId: z.string().uuid(),
230		projectId: z.string().uuid(),
231		name: z.string().trim().min(1),
232		createdAt: persistedDateSchema,
233		tabOrder: z.number().int().default(0),
234		isCollapsed: z.boolean().default(false),
235		color: z.string().nullable().default(null),
236	});
237	
238	const v2ExecutionModeSchema = z.enum([
239		"split-pane",
240		"new-tab",
241		"new-tab-split-pane",
242		"sequential",
243	]);
244	
245	// projectIds uses plain z.string() (not uuid) because v1 accepts arbitrary
246	// string IDs and the migration copies them verbatim.
247	export const v2TerminalPresetSchema = z.object({
248		id: z.string().uuid(),
249		name: z.string(),
250		description: z.string().optional(),
251		cwd: z.string().default(""),
252		commands: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
253		projectIds: z.array(z.string()).nullable().default(null),
254		pinnedToBar: z.boolean().optional(),
255		useAsWorkspaceRun: z.boolean().optional(),
256		applyOnWorkspaceCreated: z.boolean().optional(),
257		applyOnNewTab: z.boolean().optional(),
258		executionMode: v2ExecutionModeSchema.default("new-tab"),
259		tabOrder: z.number().int().default(0),
260		createdAt: persistedDateSchema,
261		// When set, the preset is live-linked to a host-service agent config id.
262		// Older rows may still contain a builtin preset id; the launcher/editor
263		// support that as a fallback. The stored `commands` array is a snapshot
264		// fallback for when the agent is missing or disabled.
265		agentId: z.string().optional(),
266	});
267	
268	export type DashboardSidebarProjectRow = z.infer<
269		typeof dashboardSidebarProjectSchema
270	>;
271	export type WorkspaceLocalStateRow = z.infer<typeof workspaceLocalStateSchema>;
272	export type WorkspaceRunState = z.infer<typeof workspaceRunStateSchema>;
273	export type WorkspaceRunTerminalState = z.infer<
274		typeof workspaceRunTerminalStateSchema
275	>;
276	export type DashboardSidebarSectionRow = z.infer<
277		typeof dashboardSidebarSectionSchema
278	>;
279	export type V2TerminalPresetRow = z.infer<typeof v2TerminalPresetSchema>;
280	
281	/**
282	 * Singleton row of v2 user-scoped preferences.
283	 *
284	 * fileLinks / urlLinks / sidebarFileLinks map click tiers
285	 * (plain, ⇧, ⌘, ⌘⇧) to an action:
286	 *   - null        → tier is unbound (surfaces show a hint or no-op)
287	 *   - "pane"      → open in current tab/pane (file viewer, in-app browser)
288	 *   - "newTab"    → open in a new tab/pane
289	 *   - "external"  → open in the external app (editor / system browser)
290	 *
291	 * Surfaces:
292	 *   - fileLinks / urlLinks: links embedded in terminal output and markdown.
293	 *     Terminal reads all 4 tiers; 2-tier surfaces (chat, task markdown)
294	 *     collapse shift→plain and metaShift→meta.
295	 *   - sidebarFileLinks: file rows in the sidebar (tree, changes, diff header).
296	 *
297	 * portOpenAction is a single action (not a tier map) for detected-port
298	 * badges: "pane" = in-app browser, "newTab" = new in-app tab,
299	 * "external" = system browser.
300	 *
301	 * Resolution and labels live in src/renderer/lib/clickPolicy.
302	 */
303	const linkActionSchema = z.enum(["pane", "newTab", "external"]);
304	
305	export type LinkAction = z.infer<typeof linkActionSchema>;
306	
307	const linkTierMapSchema = z.object({
308		plain: linkActionSchema.nullable(),
309		shift: linkActionSchema.nullable(),
310		meta: linkActionSchema.nullable(),
311		metaShift: linkActionSchema.nullable(),
312	});
313	
314	export type LinkTierMap = z.infer<typeof linkTierMapSchema>;
315	export type LinkTier = keyof LinkTierMap;
316	
317	const DEFAULT_LINK_TIER_MAP: LinkTierMap = {
318		plain: null,
319		shift: null,
320		meta: "pane",
321		metaShift: "external",
322	};
323	
324	const LEGACY_SIDEBAR_FILE_LINKS: LinkTierMap = {
325		plain: "pane",
326		shift: "newTab",
327		meta: "external",
328		metaShift: "external",
329	};
330	
331	const DEFAULT_SIDEBAR_FILE_LINKS: LinkTierMap = {
332		plain: "pane",
333		shift: "newTab",
334		meta: "pane",
335		metaShift: "external",
336	};
337	
338	// Clicking a port badge's open affordance opens http://localhost:<port>.
339	// A single action chooses where: "pane" = in-app browser, "newTab" = new
340	// in-app tab, "external" = system browser.
341	const DEFAULT_PORT_OPEN_ACTION: LinkAction = "external";
342	
343	function isSameLinkTierMap(a: LinkTierMap, b: LinkTierMap): boolean {
344		return (
345			a.plain === b.plain &&
346			a.shift === b.shift &&
347			a.meta === b.meta &&
348			a.metaShift === b.metaShift
349		);
350	}
351	
352	function isCompleteLinkTierMap(
353		value: Partial<LinkTierMap>,
354	): value is LinkTierMap {
355		return (
356			"plain" in value &&
357			"shift" in value &&
358			"meta" in value &&
359			"metaShift" in value
360		);
361	}
362	
363	export const v2UserPreferencesSchema = z.object({
364		id: z.literal("preferences"),
365		fileLinks: linkTierMapSchema.default(DEFAULT_LINK_TIER_MAP),
366		urlLinks: linkTierMapSchema.default(DEFAULT_LINK_TIER_MAP),
367		sidebarFileLinks: linkTierMapSchema.default(DEFAULT_SIDEBAR_FILE_LINKS),
368		portOpenAction: linkActionSchema.default(DEFAULT_PORT_OPEN_ACTION),
369		terminalPresetsInitialized: z.boolean().default(false),
370		rightSidebarOpen: z.boolean().default(true),
371		rightSidebarTab: z.enum(["changes", "files"]).default("changes"),
372		rightSidebarWidth: z.number().default(340),
373		deleteLocalBranch: z.boolean().default(false),
374		showPresetsBar: z.boolean().default(true),
375	});
376	
377	export type V2UserPreferencesRow = z.infer<typeof v2UserPreferencesSchema>;
378	
379	export const V2_USER_PREFERENCES_ID = "preferences" as const;
380	
381	export const DEFAULT_V2_USER_PREFERENCES: V2UserPreferencesRow = {
382		id: V2_USER_PREFERENCES_ID,
383		fileLinks: DEFAULT_LINK_TIER_MAP,
384		urlLinks: DEFAULT_LINK_TIER_MAP,
385		sidebarFileLinks: DEFAULT_SIDEBAR_FILE_LINKS,
386		portOpenAction: DEFAULT_PORT_OPEN_ACTION,
387		terminalPresetsInitialized: false,
388		rightSidebarOpen: true,
389		rightSidebarTab: "changes",
390		rightSidebarWidth: 340,
391		deleteLocalBranch: false,
392		showPresetsBar: true,
393	};
394	
395	/**
396	 * Heal a stored workspaceLocalState row against current defaults. Identity
397	 * fields (workspaceId, projectId, paneLayout, createdAt) pass through from
398	 * the stored row — they have no synthesizable default. Optional fields with
399	 * intrinsic defaults get filled at both the top level and inside sidebarState.
400	 */
401	export function healWorkspaceLocalState(raw: unknown): WorkspaceLocalStateRow {
402		const r = (
403			raw && typeof raw === "object" ? raw : {}
404		) as Partial<WorkspaceLocalStateRow>;
405		const sidebar = (
406			r.sidebarState && typeof r.sidebarState === "object" ? r.sidebarState : {}
407		) as Partial<WorkspaceLocalStateRow["sidebarState"]>;
408		return {
409			...r,
410			// Heal a malformed/legacy persisted layout so consumers never render an
411			// undefined node. Passed through untouched before, which white-screened
412			// the workspace view on a corrupt layout.
413			paneLayout: sanitizePaneLayout(r.paneLayout),
414			viewedFiles:
415				r.viewedFiles ?? WORKSPACE_LOCAL_STATE_OPTIONAL_DEFAULTS.viewedFiles,
416			recentlyViewedFiles:
417				r.recentlyViewedFiles ??
418				WORKSPACE_LOCAL_STATE_OPTIONAL_DEFAULTS.recentlyViewedFiles,
419			workspaceRunTerminals:
420				r.workspaceRunTerminals ??
421				WORKSPACE_LOCAL_STATE_OPTIONAL_DEFAULTS.workspaceRunTerminals,
422			sidebarState: {
423				...SIDEBAR_STATE_DEFAULTS,
424				...sidebar,
425			} as WorkspaceLocalStateRow["sidebarState"],
426		} as WorkspaceLocalStateRow;
427	}
428	
429	/**
430	 * Heal a stored v2 user-preferences row against current defaults. Used by the
431	 * localStorage collection's read-time parser so rows persisted before a field
432	 * was added (top-level or nested in a LinkTierMap) don't surface as undefined
433	 * to consumers. Per-tier defaults vary by map, so we deep-merge each tier map
434	 * against its own default rather than relying on a single Zod default.
435	 */
436	export function healV2UserPreferences(raw: unknown): V2UserPreferencesRow {
437		const r = (
438			raw && typeof raw === "object" ? raw : {}
439		) as Partial<V2UserPreferencesRow>;
440		const sidebarFileLinks = r.sidebarFileLinks
441			? {
442					...DEFAULT_V2_USER_PREFERENCES.sidebarFileLinks,
443					...r.sidebarFileLinks,
444				}
445			: DEFAULT_V2_USER_PREFERENCES.sidebarFileLinks;
446		const shouldMigrateLegacySidebarFileLinks =
447			r.sidebarFileLinks &&
448			isCompleteLinkTierMap(r.sidebarFileLinks) &&
449			isSameLinkTierMap(r.sidebarFileLinks, LEGACY_SIDEBAR_FILE_LINKS);
450		return {
451			...DEFAULT_V2_USER_PREFERENCES,
452			...r,
453			fileLinks: { ...DEFAULT_V2_USER_PREFERENCES.fileLinks, ...r.fileLinks },
454			urlLinks: { ...DEFAULT_V2_USER_PREFERENCES.urlLinks, ...r.urlLinks },
455			sidebarFileLinks: shouldMigrateLegacySidebarFileLinks
456				? DEFAULT_V2_USER_PREFERENCES.sidebarFileLinks
457				: sidebarFileLinks,
458		};
459	}
460	
461	export type WorkspacesCreateInput =
462		inferRouterInputs<AppRouter>["workspaces"]["create"];
463	
464	export const failedWorkspaceCreateSchema = z.object({
465		id: z.string().uuid(),
466		hostId: z.string(),
467		input: z.custom<WorkspacesCreateInput>(),
468		error: z.string(),
469		failedAt: persistedDateSchema,
470	});
471	
472	export type FailedWorkspaceCreateRow = z.infer<
473		typeof failedWorkspaceCreateSchema
474	>;
475	
476	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
477	// (KANBAN) Local-only board: columns + cards.
478	//
479	// A single device-local Kanban that mirrors every branch (workspace) as a card
480	// plus user-created "Queued" task cards. Local-only like every other v2 sidebar
481	// UI-state collection — no server/Electric/Drizzle, created on first use, healed
482	// on read. See feature_plan_kanban-board.html.
483	//
484	// Snooze/archive: for a BOUND card (workspaceId set) the visibility comes from
485	// the branch's sidebarState (one source of truth) — the card's own snooze/
486	// archive fields are used ONLY for unbound (Queued) cards.
487	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
488	
489	export const kanbanColumnSchema = z.object({
490		id: z.string(),
491		name: z.string().default(""),
492		tabOrder: z.number().int().default(0),
493		// Exactly one column is the fixed first "Queued" column (unbound tasks only).
494		// Healed/seeded with a deterministic id (see kanbanQueueColumnId).
495		isQueue: z.boolean().default(false),
496		// Exactly one column is the fixed FINAL "Completed" column. Cards dropped
497		// there are stamped done (see card.completedAt); the column can't be moved
498		// or deleted. Healed/seeded from KANBAN_COMPLETED_COLUMN_ID like the queue.
499		isCompleted: z.boolean().default(false),
500		// (KANBAN COMPLETED) Per-column completed-date filter — only meaningful on
501		// the Completed column. "last-month" = previous calendar month; "custom"
502		// uses the local-midnight from/to bounds (inclusive days).
503		completedFilter: z.enum(["all", "last-month", "custom"]).default("all"),
504		completedFilterFrom: z.number().nullable().default(null),
505		completedFilterTo: z.number().nullable().default(null),
506		// Display-only sort. NEVER rewrites card tabOrder — flipping back to "manual"
507		// restores the manual drag order untouched.
508		sortMode: z.enum(["manual", "deadline"]).default("manual"),
509		// Per-column Snoozed / Archived section reveal + collapse (mirrors the
510		// project-level section flags on dashboardSidebarProjectSchema).
511		showSnoozed: z.boolean().default(false),
512		showArchived: z.boolean().default(false),
513		snoozedCollapsed: z.boolean().default(false),
514		archivedCollapsed: z.boolean().default(false),
515		// (RECYCLE-BIN) Per-column reveal + collapse for the Recycle Bin sub-section
516		// (soft-deleted cards), mirroring the Snoozed / Archived flags above.
517		showRecycleBin: z.boolean().default(false),
518		recycleBinCollapsed: z.boolean().default(false),
519		createdAt: z.number().default(0),
520	});
521	
522	export type KanbanColumnRow = z.infer<typeof kanbanColumnSchema>;
523	
524	export const kanbanCardSchema = z.object({
525		// BOUND cards use a deterministic id `workspace:<workspaceId>` so reconcile /
526		// promote / merge can never create two cards for one branch. Queued
527		// (unbound) cards use a uuid.
528		id: z.string(),
529		columnId: z.string(),
530		tabOrder: z.number().int().default(0),
531		title: z.string().default(""),
532		description: z.string().nullable().default(null),
533		// Date-only deadline stored as local-midnight epoch-ms (null = none).
534		deadline: z.number().nullable().default(null),
535		// (DEADLINE-TIE-ORDER) Manual order WITHIN a deadline tie group (same due
536		// day, or the no-deadline tail) while the column is deadline-sorted. Kept
537		// separate from tabOrder so deadline-mode drags never scramble the manual
538		// order (and vice versa). null = never ordered there → renders BELOW the
539		// explicitly ordered cards of its group; reset to null whenever the card's
540		// deadline changes or it moves column (it "arrives" as a new item).
541		deadlineTabOrder: z.number().int().nullable().default(null),
542		// null = Queued / unbound; set = bound to a branch (workspace).
543		workspaceId: z.string().nullable().default(null),
544		// Used ONLY when unbound. Bound cards derive snooze/archive from the branch's
545		// sidebarState — these stay null for them.
546		snoozeUntil: z.number().nullable().default(null),
547		snoozeLaunchId: z.string().nullable().default(null),
548		archivedAt: z.number().nullable().default(null),
549		// (KANBAN COMPLETED) When the card was LAST dropped into the Completed
550		// column (epoch-ms; date-editable afterwards). The report datum for ALL
551		// cards — for bound cards the branch's sidebarState.completedAt is only the
552		// sidebar-visibility flag; this card field is what's displayed, filtered,
553		// sorted and (via the daily append-only backup) preserved.
554		completedAt: z.number().nullable().default(null),
555		// "project / branch" snapshot taken at completion — the subtitle a FROZEN
556		// record (completed card whose branch was later deleted) renders, since the
557		// live workspace row is gone.
558		completedContext: z.string().nullable().default(null),
559		// (RECYCLE-BIN) Soft-delete timestamp for an UNBOUND (Queued) card. Bound
560		// cards derive their bin state from the branch's sidebarState.deletedAt (one
561		// source of truth) — this stays null for them. Set by deleteCard, cleared by
562		// restoreCard; permanent removal still hard-deletes the row.
563		deletedAt: z.number().nullable().default(null),
564		createdAt: z.number().default(0),
565	});
566	
567	export type KanbanCardRow = z.infer<typeof kanbanCardSchema>;
568	
569	// Deterministic ids — the invariants ("exactly one queue", "one card per
570	// branch") are enforced by id, not just Zod defaults (localStorage reads skip
571	// defaults; see withReadHeal).
572	export const KANBAN_BOUND_CARD_PREFIX = "workspace:";
573	
574	export function kanbanBoundCardId(workspaceId: string): string {
575		return `${KANBAN_BOUND_CARD_PREFIX}${workspaceId}`;
576	}
577	
578	/** The fixed first "Queued" column id. Constant (not per-org) because the
579	 * collection's storageKey is already org-scoped, so this is unique within each
580	 * org's board. Seeded/healed to exactly one. */
581	export const KANBAN_QUEUE_COLUMN_ID = "queue";
582	
583	/** tabOrder reserved for the Queued column — always sorts first. */
584	export const KANBAN_QUEUE_TAB_ORDER = -1_000_000;
585	
586	/** The fixed FINAL "Completed" column id. Constant (not per-org) because the
587	 * collection's storageKey is already org-scoped. Seeded/healed to exactly one. */
588	export const KANBAN_COMPLETED_COLUMN_ID = "completed";
589	
590	/** tabOrder reserved for the Completed column — always sorts last. */
591	export const KANBAN_COMPLETED_TAB_ORDER = 1_000_000;
592	
593	const KANBAN_COLUMN_DEFAULTS = {
594		name: "",
595		tabOrder: 0,
596		isQueue: false,
597		isCompleted: false,
598		sortMode: "manual",
599		completedFilter: "all",
600		completedFilterFrom: null,
601		completedFilterTo: null,
602		showSnoozed: false,
603		showArchived: false,
604		snoozedCollapsed: false,
605		archivedCollapsed: false,
606		showRecycleBin: false,
607		recycleBinCollapsed: false,
608		createdAt: 0,
609	} as const;
610	
611	const KANBAN_CARD_DEFAULTS = {
612		tabOrder: 0,
613		title: "",
614		description: null,
615		deadline: null,
616		deadlineTabOrder: null,
617		workspaceId: null,
618		snoozeUntil: null,
619		snoozeLaunchId: null,
620		archivedAt: null,
621		completedAt: null,
622		completedContext: null,
623		deletedAt: null,
624		createdAt: 0,
625	} as const;
626	
627	/** Heal a stored Kanban column row — merges defaults so rows persisted before a
628	 * field existed read back normalized (identity field `id` passes through). */
629	export function healKanbanColumn(raw: unknown): KanbanColumnRow {
630		const r = (
631			raw && typeof raw === "object" ? raw : {}
632		) as Partial<KanbanColumnRow>;
633		const merged = { ...KANBAN_COLUMN_DEFAULTS, ...r } as KanbanColumnRow;
634		// Derive the queue/completed flags from the deterministic ids (don't trust
635		// the stored flags): guarantees exactly one of each and stops a stale row
636		// hydrating as the wrong kind. Queue always sorts first, Completed last.
637		merged.isQueue = merged.id === KANBAN_QUEUE_COLUMN_ID;
638		if (merged.isQueue) merged.tabOrder = KANBAN_QUEUE_TAB_ORDER;
639		merged.isCompleted = merged.id === KANBAN_COMPLETED_COLUMN_ID;
640		if (merged.isCompleted) merged.tabOrder = KANBAN_COMPLETED_TAB_ORDER;
641		return merged;
642	}
643	
644	/** Heal a stored Kanban card row — merges defaults (identity fields `id` /
645	 * `columnId` pass through from the stored row). */
646	export function healKanbanCard(raw: unknown): KanbanCardRow {
647		const r = (
648			raw && typeof raw === "object" ? raw : {}
649		) as Partial<KanbanCardRow>;
650		return { ...KANBAN_CARD_DEFAULTS, ...r } as KanbanCardRow;
651	}

  completedAt/deletedAt in the host-owned sidebar row projection
  (useDashboardSidebarData rawSidebarWorkspaces) — dropped by the v1.14.2
  upstream merge: snooze wrote but rows never left the active lane (Snoozed
  always 0), the Recycle Bin always showed empty (deleted rows misfiled under
  Archived), archived masters vanished entirely, completed threads resurfaced
  under Archived. Kanban was unaffected, so board and sidebar disagreed.
- (SNOOZE-MAIN) snoozeWorkspace now inserts a pre-snoozed local-state row for
  an auto-included main that has none yet (mirrors archiveWorkspace) — Snooze
  on a master row was a silent no-op.
- (MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY) hardening: deleteWorkspace/completeWorkspace resolve
  the workspace type host-record-first and FAIL LOUD when unresolvable (the
  cache-only guard failed open); the missing-worktree page offers Archive
  instead of Delete for mains; Empty Recycle Bin defensively skips mains.
- (REMOVE-STICKY) "Remove project from sidebar" tombstones EVERY workspace of
  the project incl. mains, and passive workspace route mounts (session
  restore, kanban split) use placeWorkspaceFromPassiveMount, which never
  re-inserts the project's sidebar row and never resurrects a hidden row — a
  removed project can only come back via an explicit open (the "PX v1 apps"
  row the user removed kept resurrecting through the passive ensure).
- (PATH-CI-DEDUPE) host-service project.findByPath / import-collision checks
  compare projects.repoPath case-insensitively on win32/darwin — a
  case-variant path missed the existing row and minted a duplicate project
  (cloud slug conflict suffixed -2 instead of reusing).
- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) nightly merge: Opus now reviews EVERY merged delta —
  clean or conflicted — against FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/
  publish. Fail closed: no parsable verdict, a BREAKAGE verdict, or a review
  run that modified the tree all hard-abort with the baseline untouched. This
  is the gate the v1.14.2 projection incident needed: a cleanly-merging
  upstream hunk that semantically breaks a fork feature with zero conflicts.

FEATURES.md gains marker rows for all of the above; AGENTS.md gate
description updated. sidebarMutations tests updated for main tombstoning
(6/6 pass); full marker gate verified locally.

content.content: Launching skill: code-review

content.text:
`low effort → 1 diff pass → no verify → ≤4 findings`

## Turn 1 — read

One tool call: read the unified diff (`git diff @{upstream}...HEAD; git diff HEAD`
to cover both committed and uncommitted changes, or `git diff main...HEAD` /
the target passed as an argument). Skip test/fixture
hunks (`test/`, `spec/`, `__tests__/`, `*_test.*`, `*.test.*`,
`fixtures/`, `testdata/`) — test-file changes are not reviewed at this level.
No subagents, no full-file reads.

## Turn 2 — findings

Flag runtime-correctness bugs visible from the hunk alone: inverted/wrong
condition, off-by-one, null/undefined deref where adjacent lines show the value
can be absent, removed guard, falsy-zero check, missing `await`,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in a catch that should propagate.
Also flag — still from the hunk alone — new code that duplicates an existing
helper visible in the diff context, and dead code the diff leaves behind.

Do **not** flag style, naming, perf, missing tests, or anything outside the
hunk.

Output at most **4 findings**, most-severe first, one line each:
`path/to/file.ext:123 — what's wrong and the concrete failure`. If nothing
qualifies, output exactly `(none)`.

diff --git a/.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml b/.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml
index e5cf01676..61be5541a 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml
@@ -1,16 +1,21 @@
 name: Nightly upstream merge
 
 # The ongoing half of the architecture. Nightly: if upstream superset published a
 # newer desktop-v* tag than .fork/upstream-baseline.txt, merge that tag into the
 # fork. git's 3-way merge handles the non-conflicting majority deterministically;
 # Opus (--effort high) resolves ONLY the conflicted files, preserving every
-# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the deterministic ARM64
-# build. On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
+# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the dependency gates, then
+# (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) an Opus review of the WHOLE merged delta — clean or
+# conflicted — against the fork feature manifest (a cleanly-merging upstream hunk
+# can semantically break a fork feature with zero conflicts; v1.14.2 dropped the
+# sidebar snooze/bin fields that way). Only then the deterministic ARM64 build.
+# On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
 # fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
-# build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline untouched -> recovery loop.
+# semantic breakage verdict, build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline
+# untouched -> recovery loop.
 #
 # AI cost scales with the UPSTREAM delta per release, NOT with how large the fork
 # grows. The build itself stays 100% AI-free.
 #
 # NOTE: schedule only fires from the DEFAULT branch, so this is dormant until the
 # fork-baseline branch is promoted to main.
@@ -187,16 +192,62 @@ jobs:
               echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
             fi
             exit 1
           fi
           echo "::endgroup::"
 
+          # --- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) AI review of the WHOLE merged delta -------
+          # The marker gate only proves feature TOKENS still exist, and the
+          # build's (REFERR-GATE) only catches cannot-find-name diagnostics. A
+          # cleanly-merging upstream hunk can still SEMANTICALLY break a fork
+          # feature with zero conflicts — v1.14.2 rebuilt the sidebar row
+          # projection in a non-conflicting hunk and silently dropped the fork's
+          # snooze/archive/completed/deleted fields: merged clean, compiled
+          # clean, broke Snooze + Recycle Bin + archived masters. EVERY merge —
+          # clean or conflicted — is therefore reviewed by Opus against
+          # FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/publish. Fail CLOSED: no
+          # parsable verdict (rate limit, malformed output) aborts rather than
+          # ships an unreviewed merge; a BREAKAGE verdict hard-aborts with the
+          # findings in the log (baseline untouched -> recovery loop).
+          echo "::group::semantic feature review (Opus)"
+          npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
+          REVIEW_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt"
+          rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"
+          # The reviewer must not modify the tree; snapshot the work-tree state
+          # and refuse to continue if the review run changed ANYTHING.
+          STATUS_BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
+          REVIEW_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are reviewing an ALREADY-RESOLVED, staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} into our Windows-ARM64 fork. Your ONLY job is to decide whether the merged result SEMANTICALLY breaks any fork feature. Steps: (1) Read FEATURES.md — the fork feature manifest (feature descriptions + marker tokens). (2) Run 'git --no-pager diff --cached --name-only' to list every file the merge changed. (3) For each changed file that participates in a fork feature — it contains a FEATURES.md marker token, or it is in the blast radius of one (e.g. a projection/select/mapping that a feature's fields flow through, a component a feature's props pass through, a hook a feature's guard lives in) — read the staged diff ('git --no-pager diff --cached -- <file>') AND the surrounding file content, and verify the fork behaviour still works end-to-end: fork-added fields still projected/carried, fork branches and guards still reachable, fork props still passed, fork call sites still invoked. An upstream refactor that preserves behaviour is fine; flag only real breakage. (4) Write your verdict to the file at ${REVIEW_FILE} — the FIRST line must be exactly 'VERDICT: OK' or 'VERDICT: BREAKAGE'; for BREAKAGE add one line per finding: '<file> — <feature/marker> — <what broke>'. Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. Output nothing else."
+          attempt=1
+          while [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
+            echo "::group::claude review attempt $attempt"
+            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REVIEW_PROMPT" || true
+            echo "::endgroup::"
+            if [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] && head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then break; fi
+            attempt=$((attempt+1))
+          done
+          STATUS_AFTER=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
+          if [ "$STATUS_BEFORE" != "$STATUS_AFTER" ]; then
+            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) the review run modified the work tree — refusing to trust or commit it. Baseline untouched."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          if ! [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] || ! head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then
+            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) no parsable review verdict for $NEWTAG — refusing to ship an UNREVIEWED merge (fail closed). Baseline untouched."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          echo "--- review verdict ---"
+          cat "$REVIEW_FILE"
+          if head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -q 'BREAKAGE'; then
+            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus found fork-feature breakage in the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Findings above. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          echo "::endgroup::"
+
           # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
           printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
           git add -A
-          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present."
+          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present; merged delta passed the (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus feature review."
           # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
           # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
           # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
           # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
           # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
           # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
diff --git a/AGENTS.md b/AGENTS.md

+# can semantically break a fork feature with zero conflicts; v1.14.2 dropped the
+# sidebar snooze/bin fields that way). Only then the deterministic ARM64 build.
+# On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
 # fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
-# build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline untouched -> recovery loop.
+# semantic breakage verdict, build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline
+# untouched -> recovery loop.
 #
 # AI cost scales with the UPSTREAM delta per release, NOT with how large the fork
 # grows. The build itself stays 100% AI-free.
 #
 # NOTE: schedule only fires from the DEFAULT branch, so this is dormant until the
 # fork-baseline branch is promoted to main.
@@ -187,16 +192,62 @@ jobs:
               echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
             fi
             exit 1
           fi
           echo "::endgroup::"
 
+          # --- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) AI review of the WHOLE merged delta -------
+          # The marker gate only proves feature TOKENS still exist, and the
+          # build's (REFERR-GATE) only catches cannot-find-name diagnostics. A
+          # cleanly-merging upstream hunk can still SEMANTICALLY break a fork
+          # feature with zero conflicts — v1.14.2 rebuilt the sidebar row
+          # projection in a non-conflicting hunk and silently dropped the fork's
+          # snooze/archive/completed/deleted fields: merged clean, compiled
+          # clean, broke Snooze + Recycle Bin + archived masters. EVERY merge —
+          # clean or conflicted — is therefore reviewed by Opus against
+          # FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/publish. Fail CLOSED: no
+          # parsable verdict (rate limit, malformed output) aborts rather than
+          # ships an unreviewed merge; a BREAKAGE verdict hard-aborts with the
+          # findings in the log (baseline untouched -> recovery loop).
+          echo "::group::semantic feature review (Opus)"
+          npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
+          REVIEW_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt"
+          rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"
+          # The reviewer must not modify the tree; snapshot the work-tree state
+          # and refuse to continue if the review run changed ANYTHING.
+          STATUS_BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
+          REVIEW_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are reviewing an ALREADY-RESOLVED, staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} into our Windows-ARM64 fork. Your ONLY job is to decide whether the merged result SEMANTICALLY breaks any fork feature. Steps: (1) Read FEATURES.md — the fork feature manifest (feature descriptions + marker tokens). (2) Run 'git --no-pager diff --cached --name-only' to list every file the merge changed. (3) For each changed file that participates in a fork feature — it contains a FEATURES.md marker token, or it is in the blast radius of one (e.g. a projection/select/mapping that a feature's fields flow through, a component a feature's props pass through, a hook a feature's guard lives in) — read the staged diff ('git --no-pager diff --cached -- <file>') AND the surrounding file content, and verify the fork behaviour still works end-to-end: fork-added fields still projected/carried, fork branches and guards still reachable, fork props still passed, fork call sites still invoked. An upstream refactor that preserves behaviour is fine; flag only real breakage. (4) Write your verdict to the file at ${REVIEW_FILE} — the FIRST line must be exactly 'VERDICT: OK' or 'VERDICT: BREAKAGE'; for BREAKAGE add one line per finding: '<file> — <feature/marker> — <what broke>'. Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. Output nothing else."
+          attempt=1
+          while [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
+            echo "::group::claude review attempt $attempt"
+            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REVIEW_PROMPT" || true
+            echo "::endgroup::"
+            if [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] && head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then break; fi
+            attempt=$((attempt+1))
+          done
+          STATUS_AFTER=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
+          if [ "$STATUS_BEFORE" != "$STATUS_AFTER" ]; then
+            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) the review run modified the work tree — refusing to trust or commit it. Baseline untouched."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          if ! [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] || ! head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then
+            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) no parsable review verdict for $NEWTAG — refusing to ship an UNREVIEWED merge (fail closed). Baseline untouched."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          echo "--- review verdict ---"
+          cat "$REVIEW_FILE"
+          if head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -q 'BREAKAGE'; then
+            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus found fork-feature breakage in the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Findings above. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          echo "::endgroup::"
+
           # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
           printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
           git add -A
-          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present."
+          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present; merged delta passed the (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus feature review."
           # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
           # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
           # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
           # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
           # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
           # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges

index 0d903f4fa..1b76cb454 100644
--- a/AGENTS.md
+++ b/AGENTS.md
@@ -22,17 +22,22 @@ truth; we track upstream by **merging its deltas**, not by re-applying changes.
 - **Nightly (minimal AI).** `.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml` runs nightly: if
   upstream published a newer `desktop-v*` tag, it `git merge`s it in. Git resolves
   the non-conflicting majority deterministically; an **Opus** model (`--effort
   high`) resolves **only the conflicted files**, preserving every feature. AI cost
   scales with the **upstream delta per release**, NOT with how large the fork grows.
 - **Gates.** After the merge: every marker in `FEATURES.md` must still be present
-  (else a feature was dropped → reject), then the deterministic build must go green.
+  (else a feature was dropped → reject), the dependency gates run, then
+  `(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE)`: Opus reviews the WHOLE merged delta — clean or
+  conflicted — against `FEATURES.md` for semantic feature breakage (fail closed:
+  no verdict / a BREAKAGE verdict / a review that edited the tree all abort),
+  then the deterministic build must go green.
   On a clean merge + green build the nightly **auto-publishes the single
   `desktop-v<version>` Release** (rebuilt in place — see One-version below) and
-  advances the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker, build
-  failure) **hard-aborts** and leaves the baseline untouched → recovery.
+  advances the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
+  breakage verdict, build failure) **hard-aborts** and leaves the baseline
+  untouched → recovery.
 - **Recovery.** When a nightly merge can't be done cleanly, fix it locally with the
   maintainer, rebuild, re-freeze the baseline; the nightly then resumes merging only
   *new* upstream changes from that point.
 
 ## Fork non-negotiables
 
@@ -45,15 +50,16 @@ truth; we track upstream by **merging its deltas**, not by re-applying changes.
   (`scripts/check-dangerous-diagnostics.mjs`, run by the build workflow, and
   therefore by every nightly): cannot-find-name tsc diagnostics
   (TS2304/2552-class) DO fail the build — bare identifiers that throw
   ReferenceError at runtime, the exact damage a cleanly-merging upstream hunk
   leaves in fork code the AI resolver never sees (v1.14.0 layout.tsx
   incident). Validate + e2e locally before relying on a release.
-- **AI only in the nightly merge.** The build is AI-free. The nightly conflict
-  resolver needs `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`; if rate-limited it aborts rather than ship
-  a half-merged fork.

---
name: project-sidebar-v1142-regression-and-semantic-gate
description: v1.14.2 merge silently dropped sidebar-state fields (snooze/bin/archive broke); fix batch 1ac9d8482 + the new MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE (Opus reviews EVERY nightly merge)
metadata:
  type: project
---

Fix batch commit `1ac9d8482` on main (2026-07-10), all markers in FEATURES.md same-commit:

- **(SIDEBAR-STATE-PROJECTION)** — upstream 1e89c577b (host-owned workspaces, in v1.14.0/2 merge) rebuilt `rawSidebarWorkspaces` in `useDashboardSidebarData.ts` and dropped `snoozeUntil/snoozeLaunchId/archivedAt/completedAt/deletedAt` in a CLEAN (non-conflicting) hunk: snooze wrote but rows never left the active lane, Recycle Bin always empty (deleted rows misfiled under Archived), archived masters vanished, completed threads resurfaced. Kanban unaffected (reads full sidebarState) → board/sidebar disagreed. The "snooze TS2339 type debt" was actually THIS runtime regression, not debt.
- **(SNOOZE-MAIN)** — snoozeWorkspace silently no-op'd on row-less auto-included mains; now inserts a pre-snoozed row (mirrors archiveWorkspace); callers pass projectId.
- **(MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY) hardening** — deleteWorkspace/completeWorkspace resolve type host-record-FIRST and FAIL LOUD when unresolvable (cache-only guard failed open); missing-worktree page shows Archive not Delete for mains (unknown type fails safe to archive-only); Empty Recycle Bin skips mains.
- **(REMOVE-STICKY)** — user's real complaint "removed project keeps coming back": removeProjectFromSidebarState left MAIN rows alive+visible, and the passive workspace route-mount ensure (V2WorkspaceMount + v2-workspace/layout.tsx, e.g. session restore) re-inserted the project row. Now: project removal tombstones EVERY workspace incl. mains; passive mounts use `placeWorkspaceFromPassiveMount` (never re-inserts project rows, never resurrects existing rows, still inserts a row-less workspace's local-state row because pane persistence updates it in place). Explicit opens (Workspaces page/setup/import/kanban-attach) keep full ensure and resurrect.
- **(PATH-CI-DEDUPE)** — host-service `projects.repoPath` dedupe was BINARY-collation exact; case-variant path → duplicate project minted (cloud slug conflict suffixes `-2` instead of reusing — `createCloudProjectWithSlugRetry` is the duplicate factory). Now lower() compare on win32/darwin.
- **(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE)** — per user directive "no automatic straight rebase; every upstream merge goes through an AI review gate": nightly-merge.yml now runs Opus (effort high) over the WHOLE staged merged delta (clean or conflicted) against FEATURES.md before commit/build/publish. Verdict file protocol (`VERDICT: OK|BREAKAGE` first line, $RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt, 2 attempts), fail CLOSED (no verdict / breakage / review modified the tree → abort, baseline untouched; tree-modification check = sha256 of `git status --porcelain` before/after).

**Duplicate "PX V1 apps" diagnosis (user's sidebar)**: NOT one project twice — two real cloud v2_projects rows: `08b2348e` "PX v1 apps" slug px-v1-apps (old non-git plain folder `C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\PX v1 apps`, 1 main workspace a74126d3) and `59ba6afd` "PX V1 apps" slug px-v1-apps-2 (the multi-repo project, 8 members, 23 worktrees). The old one's v2SidebarProjects row was re-inserted 2026-07-10T18:05 (passive resurrection above). Remedy: user removes it from sidebar AFTER installing the fixed build (removal now sticks). Data locations: cloud cache `~/.superset/tanstack-db.sqlite` (tables per collection_registry), host registry `~/.superset/host/<orgId>/host.db`, sidebar-local collections in `%APPDATA%\Superset\Partitions\superset\Local Storage\leveldb` (keys `v2-sidebar-projects-<org>` / `v2-workspace-local-state-<org>`). Orphan local-state row ab12cf91 (project 59ba) has no matching workspace — harmless, join drops it.

[[project_typecheck_debt_and_pipe_exit_trap]] [[feedback_full_gh_build_no_hot_patches]]

1	---
2	name: project-typecheck-debt-and-pipe-exit-trap
3	description: "Fork carries pre-existing tsc errors (none gate the build); `cmd | tail` masks exit codes — check PIPESTATUS"
4	metadata: 
5	  node_type: memory
6	  type: project
7	  originSessionId: 3168bd80-be8a-43d5-b3f8-b50996616bdb
8	---
9	
10	As of 2026-07-10 (post desktop-v1.14.2 merge) the fork has PRE-EXISTING typecheck debt — none of it gates anything (build = esbuild only) and none of it is a runtime regression:
11	
12	- host-service (6 errors): terminal.ts ×4 + event-bus.ts:182 — `Omit<TerminalLifecycleMessage,"type">` over a UNION collapses away `exitCode` (Omit is non-distributive); workspace-cleanup.ts:348 `git: never` from `NonNullable<Awaited<ReturnType<typeof ctx.git>>>` resolving to never. All in fork dot/cleanup code; runtime proven fine in shipped builds.
13	- desktop: `@lydell/node-pty` `node-pty.d.ts is not a module` (ARM64 pty substitution, several files); snooze/recycle-bin field reads (`snoozeUntil`/`deletedAt`) TS2339 in useDashboardSidebarData — fields flow at runtime via the local sidebar collection merge, types never modelled it.
14	- `@superset/pty-daemon` (new upstream pkg in 1.14.2) fails `build:types` (implicit any) and blocks `turbo typecheck` graph-wide — bypass with direct `bunx tsc --noEmit` in the package you care about.
15	
16	**Why:** repeatedly re-diagnosing these as "merge regressions" wastes an hour per session; they predate any given change.
17	
18	**How to apply:** when validating a change, grep tsc output for YOUR files only; ignore the debt list above (or fix it as its own batch). TRAP: `bun run typecheck 2>&1 | tail -40` returns TAIL's exit code (0) — a Bash pipeline masks the real status; use `${PIPESTATUS[0]}` or run unpiped. This false-passed a whole typecheck once. Related: [[project_nightly_build_dependency_drift]]
19	
20	**CLEAN-HUNK MERGE DRIFT (2026-07-10 incident):** the v1.14.0 nightly merge renamed layout.tsx's workspace source (`workspaces` → `hostWorkspaces` via useHostWorkspaces) in a hunk Git merged CLEANLY, so the AI conflict resolver never saw the fork's cache-first-hold lines still referencing the old identifier → `ReferenceError: workspaces is not defined`, every v2 workspace view crashed; latent through 1.14.0 AND 1.14.2 exes (esbuild bundles bare identifiers; FEATURES.md marker gate saw the feature "present"). Fixed 719cf3c74. Detection that works: per-package direct `bunx tsc --noEmit` + grep the ReferenceError class ONLY — TS2304/TS2552/TS2662/TS2663/TS18004 — the known debt contains ZERO of those codes, so this filtered gate has no false positives. (`turbo typecheck` is blocked graph-wide by @superset/pty-daemon build:types.) Known residual (follow-up, pre-existing): remote-host row transiently dropping from v2Hosts makes useHostWorkspaces isReady vacuously true → open workspace can flash not-found instead of holding; minimal fix = also hold when remembered workspace's hostId is unreachable via cache.resolveHostUrl.
