As of 2026-07-10 (post desktop-v1.14.2 merge) the fork has PRE-EXISTING typecheck debt — none of it gates anything (build = esbuild only) and none of it is a runtime regression:

- host-service (6 errors): terminal.ts ×4 + event-bus.ts:182 — `Omit<TerminalLifecycleMessage,"type">` over a UNION collapses away `exitCode` (Omit is non-distributive); workspace-cleanup.ts:348 `git: never` from `NonNullable<Awaited<ReturnType<typeof ctx.git>>>` resolving to never. All in fork dot/cleanup code; runtime proven fine in shipped builds.
- desktop: `@lydell/node-pty` `node-pty.d.ts is not a module` (ARM64 pty substitution, several files); snooze/recycle-bin field reads (`snoozeUntil`/`deletedAt`) TS2339 in useDashboardSidebarData — fields flow at runtime via the local sidebar collection merge, types never modelled it.
- `@superset/pty-daemon` (new upstream pkg in 1.14.2) fails `build:types` (implicit any) and blocks `turbo typecheck` graph-wide — bypass with direct `bunx tsc --noEmit` in the package you care about.

**Why:** repeatedly re-diagnosing these as "merge regressions" wastes an hour per session; they predate any given change.

**How to apply:** when validating a change, grep tsc output for YOUR files only; ignore the debt list above (or fix it as its own batch). TRAP: `bun run typecheck 2>&1 | tail -40` returns TAIL's exit code (0) — a Bash pipeline masks the real status; use `${PIPESTATUS[0]}` or run unpiped. This false-passed a whole typecheck once. Related: [[project_nightly_build_dependency_drift]]

**CLEAN-HUNK MERGE DRIFT (2026-07-10 incident):** the v1.14.0 nightly merge renamed layout.tsx's workspace source (`workspaces` → `hostWorkspaces` via useHostWorkspaces) in a hunk Git merged CLEANLY, so the AI conflict resolver never saw the fork's cache-first-hold lines still referencing the old identifier → `ReferenceError: workspaces is not defined`, every v2 workspace view crashed; latent through 1.14.0 AND 1.14.2 exes (esbuild bundles bare identifiers; FEATURES.md marker gate saw the feature "present"). Fixed 719cf3c74. Detection that works: per-package direct `bunx tsc --noEmit` + grep the ReferenceError class ONLY — TS2304/TS2552/TS2662/TS2663/TS18004 — the known debt contains ZERO of those codes, so this filtered gate has no false positives. (`turbo typecheck` is blocked graph-wide by @superset/pty-daemon build:types.) Known residual (follow-up, pre-existing): remote-host row transiently dropping from v2Hosts makes useHostWorkspaces isReady vacuously true → open workspace can flash not-found instead of holding; minimal fix = also hold when remembered workspace's hostId is unreachable via cache.resolveHostUrl.

lastPrompt: work on master only ok - anyway [Image #1] Why is PX V1 apps showing twice in my sidebar? I just reinstalled the latest version of SuperSet, and now this is happening. Also, I can't seem to snooze cer…

content.text:
All six fixes shipped to main (`1ac9d8482`), build running, watcher armed. Summary:

**Duplicate "PX V1 apps"** — not a render bug: TWO real cloud projects. `PX V1 apps` (9 Jun) = your multi-repo project (8 repos, 23 branches). `PX v1 apps` (29 May) = old plain-folder project for `C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\PX v1 apps`, superseded. Root cause of it "coming back after you removed it": project removal left the master's sidebar row alive, and any passive workspace route mount (e.g. session restore after reinstall) silently re-inserted the project row. Fixed — **(REMOVE-STICKY)**: removal now tombstones every workspace incl. master, and passive mounts can never re-insert a removed project; only an explicit open (Workspaces page) brings it back. After installing the new build, remove the lower "PX v1 apps" row once — it stays gone.

**Snooze broken** — v1.14.2 upstream merge regression: a cleanly-merged hunk dropped `snoozeUntil/archivedAt/completedAt/deletedAt` from the sidebar row projection, so snooze wrote but rows never left the active lane, Recycle Bin always showed empty, archived masters vanished, completed cards misfiled. Fixed **(SIDEBAR-STATE-PROJECTION)**. Also fixed the separate silent no-op snoozing master rows **(SNOOZE-MAIN)** — masters are now snoozeable, per your choice.

**Master never deletable** — was already enforced (MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY); hardened the 3 gaps (missing-worktree page now offers Archive for masters; delete/complete guards fail loud instead of open on a cold cache; Empty Recycle Bin skips masters). Plus **(PATH-CI-DEDUPE)**: case-insensitive repo-path matching so a case-only rename can't mint a duplicate project again.

**Your merge-gate directive** — done: **(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE)** in `nightly-merge.yml`. Every upstream merge — even a fully clean one with zero conflicts — is now reviewed by Opus against FEATURES.md before it can commit/build/publish. Fail closed: no verdict, a breakage verdict, or a review that touched the tree all hard-abort with the baseline untouched. This is exactly the gate that would have caught the snooze regression.

Verified: `/code-review low` clean, 6/6 sidebar tests pass, full marker gate passes, workflow YAML + bash syntax valid. [Build 29116829509](https://github.com/khairm/superset-windows-arm64/actions/runs/29116829509) publishing `desktop-v1.14.2` in place — I'll ask for your go on install timing when it's green.
